Title: Machine Learning Engineer Graduate (Trust and Safety Engineering) - 2027 Start
Description:
Team Introduction
Building a world where people can safely discover, create and connect. The Trust & Safety (T&S) team at TikTok helps ensure that our global online community is safe and empowered to create and enjoy content across all of our applications. We have invested heavily in human and machine-based moderation to remove harmful content quickly and often before it reaches our general community.

We are looking for talented individuals to join our team in 2027. As a graduate, you will get opportunities to pursue bold ideas, tackle complex challenges, and unlock limitless growth. Launch your career where inspiration is infinite at our Company.

Successful candidates must be able to commit to an onboarding date by end of year 2027. Please state your availability and graduation date clearly in your resume.

Candidates can apply to a maximum of two positions and will be considered for jobs in the order you apply. The application limit is applicable to our Company and its affiliates' jobs globally. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis - we encourage you to apply early.

As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll have the chance to work with our clients and teams to address key business problems and identify areas of growth for the company. With your education and experience, you will be able to take on real-world challenges from day one.

Responsibilities

Work with our world-class engineers to build industry-leading trust and safety systems for TikTok
Develop and build up highly-scalable classifiers, tools, models and algorithms leveraging cutting-edge machine learning, computer vision and data mining technologies
Improve our trust and safety strategy and work on model iterations
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to protect TikTok globally

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:

Individuals who are completing or have recently completed a Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Software Development, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
Solid knowledge in at least one of the following areas: machine learning, pattern recognition, NLP, data mining, or computer vision
Firm understanding of data structures and algorithms
Great communication and teamwork skills

Preferred Qualifications:

Passion about techniques and solving challenging problems
Previous experience in applications of machine learning, pattern recognition, NLP, data mining, or computer vision 
Currently pursuing your PhD or Master degree in Computer Science or related engineering field.

By submitting an application for this role, you accept and agree to our global applicant privacy policy, which may be accessed here: https://careers.tiktok.com/legal/privacy.

If you have any questions, please reach out to us at apa••••••••••••••@tiktok.com

Title: Graduate AI Engineer
Description:
EARTH AI is a fast-growing tech startup transforming the way critical metals are discovered. Founded in 2016 in Sydney, we are on a mission to secure the future of technology and infrastructure by responsibly sourcing the metals essential for global advancement. Using cutting-edge technology, we identify and develop new metal deposits with unmatched efficiency and sustainability. Our vision is to lead the industry by discovering and owning the majority of future metal deposits while becoming the most advanced, efficient, and responsible metal commodity company in the world.

About the Role

Earth AI is hiring Graduate AI Engineers to support the development of industry-leading applied AI/ML systems for mineral exploration.

This role focuses on building and improving models and agents using real-world geoscience datasets and contributing to modular AI workflows that support company-wide decision-making.

You will work closely with our Senior AI researcher, data engineers and geoscientists to develop practical, production-oriented AI components that are actively used across the business.

This is a hands-on role suited to candidates who are motivated to solve complex problems, take ownership of their work, and operate effectively in an evolving, high-impact environment.

Key responsibilities:

Develop and evaluate machine learning models/agents on:

geochemical data

geophysical data

operational and portfolio data

reports and unstructured sources

Be responsible for specialised modular AI components, in the form of AI- agents and/or classical ML models.

Contribute to the development of systems that integrate multiple models and data sources into cohesive workflows.

Gather requirements, build domain understanding, run experiments, validate outputs, and iterate based on results.

Work with real-world, imperfect datasets and translate them into usable model inputs.

Write clean, maintainable Python code and contribute to shared codebases and internal tooling.




About You

Degree in:

Computer Science (AI/ML/Data specialisation only)

Software Engineering (AI/ML/Data specialisation only)

Information Technology (AI/ML/Data specialisation only)

Artificial Intelligence

Data Science

or an AI-related quantitative/engineering discipline

Strong understanding of:

Machine learning fundamentals

Statistics and data analysis

Agentic AI and LLMs

Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), Multi-agent collaboration (MAC) and Domain-specific fine-tuning

Proficiency in Python, including NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch, etc.

Experience working with structured and/or unstructured datasets under real-world constraints (incomplete, noisy, or inconsistent data)

Familiarity with Software Development practices and Service-oriented modular architectures

Demonstrated ability to reason about systems in alignment with

service/function delivery.




Preferred

Exposure to:

LLMs, agents, or multi-step AI workflows

building end-to-end ML pipelines

Familiarity with:

version control (Git)

software engineering tools and workflows

integration of AI outcomes into software products

Hands-on experience with applying emerging AI/Data technologies to real-world applications.




What We Look For

We are looking for candidates who demonstrate:

Self-motivation – able to take initiative and drive work forward without constant direction

Critical thinking – capable of questioning assumptions and reasoning through complex problems

Attention to detail – especially when working with noisy or imperfect data

Comfort with ambiguity – able to operate effectively without fully defined problems or solutions

Ownership mindset – takes responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks

Growth orientation – motivated to learn quickly and improve through hands-on work




Why Join Us:

Growth Opportunities: We support professional development and career advancement;

Collaborative Environment: Work with a team of passionate and supportive colleagues;

Competitive compensation package, plus quarterly performance bonus;

Team lunches;

Company retreats.

Title: Ai Machine Learning Software Developer
Description:
About Us

We are an Australian Ag-tech company developing advanced fresh produce grading and quality assessment technology used by growers and packhouses to improve yield, consistency, and profitability. Our systems combine hardware, computer vision, and machine learning with hard real-time constraints, in real‑world agricultural environments.

We’re growing our engineering team and are looking for a Machine Learning Software Developer who wants to work on applied ML problems in real world applications.




The Role

You will design, build, and deploy machine learning models that sit at the heart of our fruit grading technology. This role is hands‑on and end‑to‑end: from data exploration and model development through to production deployment on real systems.

You’ll work closely with software engineers, hardware engineers, and product teams to deliver robust ML solutions that perform reliably in challenging, real‑world conditions.




What You’ll Bring


PhD in Computer Vision, Robotics, or related field

2 years’ industry experience (ideal: PhD + some commercial exposure)

Strong grounding in mathematics, algorithms, and 3D problem solving

Experience working with sensor data (LIDAR, cameras, etc.)

Strong Python skills (language less important than thinking ability)




Key Responsibilities

Develop and deploy machine learning and computer vision models for fresh produce grading and quality assessment

Work with image, sensor, and classification data to improve accuracy, speed, and robustness

Build and maintain ML pipelines (training, validation, inference, monitoring)

Optimise models for production environments (latency, memory, hardware constraints)

Collaborate with cross‑functional teams to integrate ML into production software

Continuously evaluate and improve model performance using real‑world data

Contribute to technical direction and best practices in ML development

Title: Graduate AI Engineer
Description:
Let’s not beat around the bush, we have a job vacancy, and we need you! We like to think of this role as an epic opportunity to bring your skills and expertise to the table, grow your career, and create some cool work stories along the way. So, before we lose you in the usual job advert fluff, we’ll get to the nuts and bolts of the opportunity…

What's on offer?

We are looking for a highly capable Graduate AI Engineer to join our Adelaide-based team within a leading electronic security integrator across Australia and New Zealand. This role offers a rare opportunity to apply artificial intelligence and machine learning techniques to real-world security challenges, particularly in video analytics and intelligent surveillance systems. 

You will work on the development, testing, training and deployment of AI-enabled solutions across CCTV cameras, Video Management Systems (VMS), and related platforms. You will collaborate closely with internal engineering teams, external technology partners and customers, contributing to cutting-edge projects that are deployed in live operational environments.

As a full-time permanent position, this role offers strong professional development pathways, exposure to advanced electronic security systems, as well as the opportunity to help build new AI-driven capabilities within a growing technology-focused organisation. 

Duties include, but not limited to:

Design, develop and maintain AI-enabled advanced analytics solutions with a focus on computer vision and video analytics.
Train, test, validate and optimise models for deployment within CCTV cameras, edge devices, along with Physical Access Control and Video Management Systems. 
Develop software components and pipelines to support AI model integration, deployment and lifecycle management.
Who’s the client?
As you may know, we (Optic Digital) are part of Optic Security Group (OSG).  We are OSG’s technology talent and IT division and with that, we have the privilege of recruiting this opportunity for them. So, you know about Optic Digital...but who are OSG? Let us tell you...

Optic Security Group is Australasia’s most technically advanced converged Enterprise Security Risk Management Group, working with over 1000 customers in both Australia & New Zealand. We design, install and maintain security solutions for Government, Defence, critical infrastructure organisations, and other enterprise customers.

Our mission is to enable our customers to achieve their missions by keeping their people, information, infrastructure, and assets safe. We provide comprehensive IP networked and integrated security systems, including but not limited to intrusion detection, access control, video surveillance and advanced analytics. We have over 30 years of proven expertise in tailoring award-winning solutions to meet a wide array of customer needs.

Keen to know more, head over to www.opticsecuritygroup.com and check them out.

What you'll bring

You'll possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills, while also possessing the ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a multidisciplinary team. You'll be curious and enthusiastic to learn new technologies and domains.

Postgraduate qualification (Graduate Diploma, Masters or equivalent) with coursework in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, or related field.
Strong foundational knowledge of machine learning principles and model development.
Demonstrated capability or academic focus in computer vision, image processing, or video analytics. 
Proficiency in one or more relevant programming languages (e.g. Python).
Familiarity with common AI/ML frameworks (e.g. PyTorch, TensorFlow, OpenCV or similar).
Understandings of software development practices, version control and testing methodologies.
Exposure to CCTV systems, Video Management Systems (VMS) or other electronic security platforms? Bonus! Experience deploying AI models to edge devices or embedded systems? Ideal!

The small print

Applications close 08.06.2026 and can only be submitted via Seek.

Please note, only applicants that are Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents will qualify for the clearance required to undertake this role.

Applicants will need to make themselves available for interviews in Adelaide to be considered.

For any questions regarding the role, please get in touch with Andrea at and•••••••@opticdigital.com.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer Intern, Trust and Safety Engineering - 2027 Start
Junior Statistical / ML Modeller
Description:
About the Role




This role sits at the heart of what we do. Our core activity is building trained statistical and machine learning models whose inputs are real-world datasets and whose outputs are other variables of interest that we use in the next stage of our core, end-to-end process. This process runs from raw data through to a complete, trained model:




data analysis  →  feature engineering & selection  →  model building & training




As a Junior Modeller you will work directly within this pipeline – taking existing datasets, developing data cleansing rules, engineering and selecting model features, and contributing to model building and selection. This is hands-on, practical work: you will be getting your hands dirty with large datasets on a daily basis, running analyses, testing ideas (most of which will not pan out), and iterating until something you develop genuinely works.

We are straightforward about the nature of this work: it is at times not glamorous, cutting-edge machine learning research, but it is what drives our success, and it requires someone who finds genuine satisfaction in doing it well and enthusiastically.




Key Responsibilities




Data Analysis & Cleansing

•      Explore, interrogate, and develop a thorough working understanding of our datasets.

•      Identify data quality issues and calculate appropriate cleansing and treatment rules.

•      Document data characteristics, anomalies, and cleansing decisions clearly and precisely.




Feature Engineering & Selection

•      Develop significant domain knowledge of our real-world modelling application.

•      Generate candidate model features from raw data through transformation, aggregation, and domain-informed derivation.

•      Apply feature selection techniques to identify the subset of features that improve model performance.

•      Maintain clear records of features tested, selection rationale, and outcomes.




Model Building & Evaluation

•      Build, train, and evaluate statistical and ML models using our internal tooling (primarily Stata, which is straightforward to learn if you have basic coding experience).

•      Contribute to model selection decisions, assessing candidate models against performance and profitability criteria.

•      Work with senior modellers to iterate on models based on evaluation results.




Collaboration & Documentation

•      Work closely with the wider modelling team, sharing findings clearly and contributing to peer review of each other’s work.

•      Maintain thorough, reproducible documentation of your analysis and modelling work.

•      Participate in team discussions about methodology, data, and results.


How to Apply

Please submit your CV and a short cover letter. In your cover letter, tell us briefly about a piece of data analysis or modelling work you have done – what you were trying to do, how you approached it, and what you found.

We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all backgrounds. Please let us know if you require any adjustments during the recruitment process.

Title: Graduate Data Engineer
Description:
We are currently seeking a Graduate in Data Engineering who is interested in building a career within an industry leading Australian Employer of Choice. This is a 12-month fixed term role, with potential to transition to a permanent position for the right candidate.

Joining NTI's Graduate team, you'll apply your academic foundations to real-world data engineering challenges, contributing to solutions that underpin NTI's analytical, actuarial and business intelligence platforms. You'll work alongside experienced engineers across IT and analytics teams, building capability across data pipelines, ETL processes, cloud data infrastructure and data warehousing. Supported by both your team and a broader Graduate cohort, you'll accelerate your growth in a collaborative environment — contributing to high-impact initiatives that drive operational efficiency and deliver high-quality, trusted data assets across the business.

About the role

Day to day, you will:

Work with the team to design, develop and test data pipelines and ETL processes that move and transform data between NTI systems, external partners and analytical platforms

Write and optimise SQL queries and scripts to extract, transform and load data, supporting data warehouse development and maintenance

Contribute to the automation of routine data processes to improve scalability and reduce manual effort

Learn to design and interact with APIs and data source endpoints, ingesting and translating data sets for use in internal modelling and reporting

Apply NTI's data governance and information security standards, and create and maintain technical documentation for data solutions

Collaborate with stakeholders across IT, Insights & Analytics, and the business to understand requirements and translate them into technical solutions

Explore and adopt modern data engineering tools, cloud platforms and best practices as part of NTI's evolving data infrastructure

What we would like to see from you

From a personal perspective, we'd love to see:

A strong hands-on attitude towards understanding new concepts and learning

Proactive planners and communicators who are not afraid of asking questions and challenging the status quo

A high level of analytical problem-solving ability

Team players who have a desire to work as part of a high performing team

From a technical perspective, we'd love to see the following in your application:

Undergraduate degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology or a related discipline with a strong academic record

Professional working experience through internships, work placements, industry projects or volunteering activities (desired but not essential)

Proven SQL programming skills and comfort working with relational databases

Familiarity with version control (Git), cloud data platforms (e.g. Azure, AWS or GCP), and data warehousing or lakehouse concepts

Exposure to ETL concepts, data pipeline tools or transformation frameworks (e.g. SSIS, dbt, or similar) will be highly regarded

Experience or familiarity with Python scripting and working with APIs or data source endpoints will be highly regarded

An understanding of data modelling principles, data governance practices, and the ability to document technical solutions clearly

What to expect when you're a part of the NTI family:

Boasting close to 50 years' experience and backed by two of Australia's largest insurers (CGU and Vero), you'll enjoy stability, a great brand reputation and a team of experts to learn from. We are committed to being a Best Employer of the Best People and as such, we offer:

A real and tangible commitment to your development and ongoing study

Access to discounts on over 350 retailers across Australia

The ability to apply for flexible working arrangements (subject to relevant NTI policies) + more!

At NTI, we're committed to providing an environment where our team members feel comfortable to be themselves and valued for their unique perspectives and contributions.

Ready to apply? We can't wait to read your application!

We'd love for you to apply with a resume and cover letter explaining why you'd like to join our Graduate team.

Our application process is tailored to each role based on the unique requirements. You may be required to complete phone and face to face interviews, and qualification and reference checks as part of the application process.

Title: Graduate Geospatial Data Engineer
Description:
Contribute to the success of A&G by assisting with the development of natural peril and geospatial solutions using robust, accurate and timely data-driven analysis and insight. 


About the role 

The Graduate Geospatial Data Engineer contributes to the success of A&G by assisting with the development of natural peril and geospatial solutions using robust, accurate and timely data driven analysis and insight. This technical role supports the pricing of our insurance products through data analysis, validation, and research of innovative data and methods to price and manage insurance risks.

Locations: Sydney or Brisbane 

To undertake tasks as directed by senior members of the team efficiently, thoroughly, with due care and minimal mistakes. This includes:

Assist in the development and maintenance of data pipelines, ELT processes, and data warehouses using BigQuery and GCP.
Build technical capability in writing and maintaining code for validating, processing, transforming, and storing large datasets from multiple sources.
Support the optimisation of data processes and validation testing for performance and efficiency under guidance.
Contribute to the implementation and maintenance of data governance principles, quality checks, error handling, and monitoring to ensure data quality and integrity.
Collaborate with data analysts, actuaries, and the Senior Data Engineer to understand business data needs and deliver technical solutions.
Assist in documenting data assets, sources, and their limitations for the Pricing team.
Assist with technical and geospatial analysis, primarily using GIS software, to support natural peril modelling and resolve specific issues.
Perform ad-hoc analysis and investigations related to natural perils and geospatial modelling as requested.
Perform other reasonable and relevant activities as requested to meet operational requirements.

What experience you’ll bring 
Qualifications and Experience 

Essential:

A degree in data / computer Science, engineering, or similar.
Solution focused with excellent attention to detail.
Competency in programming languages (e.g. Big Query, SQL, Python, R)
A keen interest and understanding of data modelling, governance, warehousing and security best practices
Self-motivated and passionate about continuous learning, with a keen interest in data innovation. 

Desirable:

Previous academic or project experience with cloud-based technologies
Interest in or exposure to geospatial concepts and software (e.g. QGIS).
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams.


Our Perks  

The benefits of working at A&G:

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Access to A&G’s employee reward and recognition platform, powered by Rewards Gateway - Enjoy a wide range of benefits, including: 

Personalised rewards and peer-to-peer recognition
Exclusive discounts and savings across more than 600 top retailers
A comprehensive recognition program that celebrates milestones and achievements—helping you feel valued every step of the way
Work flexibility - with options to work from home up to two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.

About us  

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community. 

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.  

Auto & General values individual differences and believes in fostering an inclusive culture that creates a great place to work for all. 

*A note from Auto & General to recruitment agencies: We politely ask that you avoid making any approaches or sending any unsolicited resumes to our Recruitment Team or Hiring Leaders across our business. Auto & General is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Title: 2027 NDIA Graduate Program
Description:
Our participants, Our people, Our purpose

The National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA) is the independent statutory agency responsible for implementing the world-leading National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), supporting eligible Australians with permanent and significant disability. 

After more than a decade of operation, the NDIS is in a major period of change, with a focus on improving participant experience, strengthening integrity, and ensuring long-term financial stability. The NDIA is committed to enhancing the quality of life and increasing both economic and social participation for people with disability.

A new role awaits

It is an exciting time to join the NDIA as we continue to strengthen and reform the Scheme. A key focus for the agency is enhancing service delivery and building capability in allied health to support the NDIS now and into the future.

The NDIA Graduate Program (NDIA GP) is designed to support graduates as they begin their careers in the NDIA and the Australian Public Service (APS). Through a structured learning and development journey, the program combines formal training with meaningful, hands-on experience across a range of business areas within the agency.

The program runs over 12 months and offers a wide range of opportunities to build your skills, broaden your experience, and develop the capabilities needed to thrive in a high-performing organisation. You will gain valuable insight into how the NDIA operates, while making a genuine contribution to important work that supports Australians with disability.

If you are passionate about social inclusion, value diversity, and are looking to kick-start a purposeful and rewarding career, the NDIA GP could be the perfect place to start.

The NDIA Graduate Program gives graduates the opportunity to:
contribute to meaningful work supporting Australians living with disability to have greater participation in the community and the economy
develop the foundational skills, behaviours, mindsets and networks graduates need to make a meaningful contribution to the APS, supported through a formal development framework
gain broad experience and exposure to different business areas through job rotations within the host business area and across the broader NDIA
be part of an inclusive and diverse work environment, where you’ll be mentored to build strong professional networks at the NDIA and in the APS
benefit from a competitive salary and flexible working conditions that support positive work-life balance
access an accelerated career pathway and salary progression from APS4.1 pay point on commencement to APS5.1 pay point upon successful completion of the Graduate Program.
Who we are looking for

The 2027 NDIA Graduate Program is seeking motivated graduates who are passionate about the NDIA’s purpose and committed to making a meaningful impact.

We are looking for individuals who thrive in collaborative environments and enjoy contributing as part of a diverse, high-performing team.

We value diversity in all its forms and recognise that different perspectives strengthen our ability to deliver positive outcomes for Australians with disability.

As the NDIA operates in a complex and fast-changing environment, we are seeking individuals who are adaptable, resilient, and able to respond to evolving priorities while maintaining a strong focus on outcomes.

We strongly encourage applications from people with disability; individuals from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds; people who identify as Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Transgender, Intersex, Queer, Asexual, Sistergirl, Brotherboy Plus (LGBTIQASB+); and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

We welcome applications from graduates with a wide range of academic backgrounds, including (but not limited to):

actuarial
allied health
behavioural science and psychology
business and commerce
computer science, ICT, cyber security, and software development
data and analytics
economics, accounting, and finance
engineering
government, public policy, and political science
human resources
legal
media, marketing, and communications
project and program management
risk
social sciences (including policy, research, health, disability, and social work)

In addition to your academic background, we are seeking graduates who demonstrate:

strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
adaptability and openness to change
data literacy and an ability to work with evidence-based insights
strong collaboration and communication skills
a genuine commitment to the values and purpose of the Australian Public Service (APS)
Eligibility

To be a part of the NDIA Graduate Program you must meet the following criteria:

be an Australian citizen
have completed, or be due to complete by 31 December 2026, at least an Australian Qualifications Framework Level 7 qualification (a bachelor’s degree), or an equivalent or higher qualification
have completed their most recent within the past 5 years at time of application
have and keep a valid Australian Government security clearance upon entry to the program
be willing to undergo police, character, health or other pre-employment checks
Locations

The NDIA operates more than 160 NDIA locations across Australia. Graduates have the opportunity to work from a wide range of these offices, including those in rural and remote areas as well as the following cities, towns and suburbs:

ACT – Canberra
NSW – Newcastle, Sydney, Penrith
NT – Alice Springs, Darwin
QLD – Brisbane, Robina, Townsville
SA – Adelaide
TAS – Hobart
VIC - Richmond, Geelong
WA – Perth

To learn more what it’s like to be an NDIA graduate, please visit the Meet our Graduates page. Further information about the program is also available on the NDIA Graduate Program webpage.

Successful candidates will be required to undergo a National Coordinated Criminal History Check (NCCHC), Worker Screening check and/or Working with Children Check (where required) and provide evidence of Australian Citizenship.

Applications for this role close 01st June 2026 at 11:30pm AEST

For enquiries relating to recruitment, please email: recruitment@ndis.gov.au. For questions specific to the NDIA Graduate Program, please contact GraduateProgram@ndis.gov.au

Please note that, with the exception of personal details, applications can NOT be edited or updated once submitted for consideration. Please make sure you are completely satisfied with your entire application before submitting.

NDIA is committed to supporting an inclusive and diverse workforce and welcomes and encourages applications from People with Disability (PwD), First Nations peoples, LGBTIQA+ people, people from Culturally and Linguistically Diverse backgrounds, Mature age workers, as well as young workers and supporting an inter-generational workforce and supporting gender equity and families. NDIA will provide reasonable adjustments for candidates to participate equitably in the recruitment process and discuss workplace adjustments to fulfil the inherent requirements of the role. If you require an adjustment to be made during the recruitment process (including alternate formats of the application form) or have an enquiry about the support provided, please contact NDIA Recruitment on rec••••••••@ndis.gov.au.

RecruitAbility applies to this position: The RecruitAbility scheme supports people with disability applying for jobs in the Australian Public Service (APS) by giving you a better opportunity to put forward your skills and experience during the selection process.

There are certain roles within the National Disability Insurance Agency that may require mandatory vaccination, subject to government order or directive. Evidence may be required prior to commencement.

Title: Technology Graduate - 2027 Graduate Program
Description:
Brighter futures start here! 🚀

What if your first job in tech didn’t just support a business…but helped transform how millions of people shop every single day?

At Kmart Group, we’re in the middle of one of the biggest retail transformations in Australia - and our Technology team is right at the centre of it.

With six purchases happening every second, the systems you help build, improve, or reimagine won’t sit in the background…they’ll power real experiences for real customers, in real time.

What You’ll Experience

As part of the Kmart Group Technology stream, you’ll dive into the innovation powering our stores. From smart systems to cutting-edge tools, you’ll get hands-on experience driving real change - while building a career that moves with the future.

This program is all about growth - real experience, real impact, and real opportunities. With mentorship from industry pros and a learning journey tailored to you, you’ll explore different tech spaces, find what fires you up, and carve out a career path that actually excites you.

Whether you want to lead digital transformation, deep dive into data, or build enterprise systems from the ground up, we’ve got you covered.

Rotations could include:
🛍 Digital (online and mobile apps)
☁️ Cloud platforms
🔌 Networks & infrastructure
📦 Supply chain distribution, merchandise and inventory technology
📊 Advanced analytics, automation and chatbots
🧠 Enterprise architecture
💼 Corporate systems
🛠 IT operations
🧪 Stores technology

Oh, and you’re not just growing here - this could open doors across the entire Wesfarmers Group. Big potential. Bigger future.

About the Program

Starting in February 2027, our 2-year program includes 3-4 rotations within the Technology function, with a permanent placement upon successful completion. During the program, you'll engage in practical and meaningful work that impacts our customers and allows you to grow your career through a personalised learning program. You'll be fully supported along this journey, working alongside and learning from industry experts.

Brighter futures really do start here!

About You

We’re looking for graduates who are driven, curious, and innovative! Ideally, you’ll have a degree in Engineering Software, Information Systems, Information Technology, Computer Science, or similar fields to hit the ground running.

Our values – Think Customer, Take Care, Live Integrity, Grow Together, and Reach Higher – will resonate with you deeply. You’ll enjoy being part of a growing, iconic Australian business with a fantastic, non-hierarchical, diverse, and inclusive culture.

To be eligible for our 2027 program, you'll need to:

Be an Australian or New Zealand citizen or have Australian permanent residency at the time of application.

Have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in the past 3 years (i.e., 2024, 2025, or 2026) or are due to complete your degree by the end of January 2027.

Be able to commence full-time employment in Melbourne in February 2027.

If this sounds like you, apply now!

What We Offer

Flexible hybrid working options through our Flex Where, Flex When, Flex Well policy, spending 60% of your week in our National Office, Stores, or DCs.

Great career opportunities across the broader Wesfarmers Group.

Inclusive leave and ongoing support for team members of all backgrounds and family circumstances.

Free onsite parking when you're required to come into the office.

Opportunity to participate in a bonus program.

A team member discount card for Kmart, Target, Bunnings, and Officeworks.

A free OnePass membership offering free delivery across Kmart and Target on eligible products.

Access to our awesome benefits program with exclusive discounts and benefits in entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and health.

Reflection room for prayer, meditation, or personal time.

Option to purchase shares through a salary sacrifice arrangement with Wesfarmers, Kmart Group’s parent company.

Applications close at 11:59pm on Sunday, 21st June.

A Place You Can Belong

At Kmart and Target, we celebrate the rich diversity of the communities in which we operate. We are committed to creating inclusive and safe environments where all our team members can contribute and succeed. We believe that all team members should feel valued, respected, and safe, and strive to ensure our recruitment process is accessible and welcoming, with applications encouraged from all candidates

We believe everyone should have the opportunity to be their best, whether applying for a role or working at the Kmart Group. Let us know about any workplace adjustments you need by emailing div••••••@kmart.com.au

Title: Azure AI & Machine Learning Data Engineer
Description:
We’re looking for a experienced Machine Learning Engineer to join a fast‑growing scale‑up with an unapologetically ambitious mindset. This is a team that moves fast, takes ownership, and does what it takes to deliver—without losing sight of quality or long‑term impact.

This isn’t just another role. It’s an opportunity to help level the playing field in sports tipping, applying advanced data science and AI to challenge how the industry operates today. You’ll be part of a team that’s rewriting the rules global users have played by for decades.

At XO Sports, we’re a collaborative, results‑driven group where innovation, accountability, and teamwork are core to how we operate. You’ll work alongside seasoned leaders in technology, AI, and sports analytics, contributing directly to products that reach and influence sports fans worldwide.

Our vision is bold: to empower the world to win through cutting‑edge predictive analytics and AI tools that genuinely put the odds back in users’ favour. Every team member plays a meaningful role in delivering that vision, and we believe your expertise, leadership, and passion for building impactful systems will help take XO Sports to the next level.

This is a senior role where you’ll design cutting-edge ML architecture, influence strategy, and bring your unique style and personality to a dynamic, collaborative team of Data Scientists.

Azure Data Scientist AI & Machine Learning Engineer

Key Responsibilities:

Hands-On Machine Learning & Data Visionary for developing a range of models used in key sports

Design, train, evaluate, and iterate on object detection, classification, and segmentation models for operational use.

Run structured experimentation, ablations, and performance analysis to guide model ideation, evolution in a technical data driven framework

Contribute to data engineering requirements to each model for training and testing purposes.

Benchmark, profile, and tune performance on embedded targets and support integration into mission critical workflows.

Define and maintain ML system assurance practices, including verification and validation criteria, performance baselines, data drift and regression checks.

Produce clear technical documentation covering system architecture, model behaviour, assumptions, and limitations to support auditability and operational confidence.

Design best in class Machine Learning Models that provide the business with cut edge IP 

Design and develop effective working platforms for data science workloads

Use Azure Databricks to design, deploy and tune Machine Learning experiments, models and endpoints

Research and implement appropriate ML algorithms and tools, with a focus on the Azure eco-system

Utilise predictive analytics to decipher complex data patterns, facilitating insightful, data-driven decisions

Design and evolve the ML prediction workflows for inference and model retraining within our broader Data and ML pipelines within Azure Data Factory, Databricks ecosystem.

Develop testbench systems that enable new models to be rapidly simulated for prediction accuracy against business specific metrics

Perform the evaluation of ML models for each given task, using common statistical analysis techniques 

Key Experience

5+ years practical experience as a Machine Learning Engineer

1+ years of practical experience in designing, deploying and tuning Machine Learning models

Proven experience as an AI Engineer or similar role

Working experience is using Azure Databricks, ML, AI tools and Python to deploy Machine Learning models for production services

Key Skills

Graduate or similar qualification in Data Science, AI/ML or similar

Deep knowledge of math, probability, statistics and algorithms

Azure Machine Learning Studio & workspace (AutoML and Designer)

Azure Data Stores such as SQL, Cosmos DB, Blob and Azure Files

Programming languages used for Data Engineering and Machine Learning, in Python

Familiarity with machine learning frameworks 

Data Engineering skills in transforming data

Familiarity with software engineering best practices, version control systems, and CI/CD pipelines

Suitable training and certification in data engineering, data science or related fields

Technical expertise with data models, data mining, and segmentation techniques

Hands-on expertise with ETL tools, within an Azure eco-system, such as Data Factory

Hands-on experience with SQL/NoSQL databases.

Title: Deloitte STEM Connect Virtual Experience
Description:
Studying STEM or interested in a career within the STEM space? Looking to develop your skills with real business problems? Now’s your chance! Our Deloitte STEM Connect Virtual Experience is open for enrolment.

We’re committed to giving you endless opportunities to learn and develop, so that you can thrive in your early career. That’s why we’ve designed a Virtual Experience, accessible 24/7 and open to everyone.

Complete set tasks and unlock sample answers written by our very own Consultants! By solving simulated client cases, you’ll build your experience in STEM and get a glimpse of a day-in-the-life as a Dloitte Consultant. You'll get to experience what it is like to work on a project that mirrors our teams' skills and the work they do in the real world. You will build skills in coding, data analysis, software development, cyber security and forensic technology.

The best part?

You can credit this experience on your CV and LinkedIn profile upon completing our virtual experience program!

Enrol now, and immerse yourself into our world to gain invaluable practical skills, build your confidence, and uncover the exciting opportunities we have for you at Deloitte!

Title: Backend Software Engineer Graduate (Trust and Safety Engineering) - 2027 Start
Description:
The Trust and Safety (TnS) engineering team is responsible for protecting our users from harmful content and abusive behaviours. With the continuous efforts of our trust and safety engineering team, TikTok can provide the best user experience and bring joy to everyone in the world. Our team is responsible for achieving goals by building content moderation process systems, rule engine, strategy systems, feature engine, human moderation platforms, risk insight systems and all kinds of supportive platforms across TnS organization.

We are looking for talented individuals to join our team in 2027. As a graduate, you will get opportunities to pursue bold ideas, tackle complex challenges, and unlock limitless growth. Launch your career where inspiration is infinite at our Company.

Successful candidates must be able to commit to an onboarding date by end of year 2027. Please state your availability and graduation date clearly in your resume.

Candidates can apply to a maximum of two positions and will be considered for jobs in the order you apply. The application limit is applicable to our Company and its affiliates' jobs globally. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis - we encourage you to apply early.

Online Assessment:
Candidates who pass resume screening will be invited to participate in Our Company's technical online assessment.

Responsibilities

Responsible for the research and development of trust and safety related systems and product functionalities on international live streaming products, including but not limited to: safety process, user perception, review efficiency improvement, strategy platform construction, etc;
Responsible for enhancing the safety experience of a large number of international live streaming users, and building high-concurrency, high-stability systems for higher daily active users;
In-depth exploration and analysis of business requirements and products to find solutions for live streaming safety issues.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:

Individuals who are completing or have recently completed a Bachelor’s or above degree in Software Development, Computer Science, computer Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
Proficiency in designing data structures, building algorithms, and at least one programming language: python / go / php / c++ / java.

Preferred Qualifications:

Have excellent problem analysis and driving ability, good at thinking about the underlying causes of problems, and adept at summarizing and concluding;
Great communication and teamwork skills;
Good teamwork, communication skills, and service awareness, proactive and optimistic, strong sense of responsibility and self-motivation.

By submitting an application for this role, you accept and agree to our global applicant privacy policy, which may be accessed here: https://careers.tiktok.com/legal/privacy.

If you have any questions, please reach out to us at apa••••••••••••••@tiktok.com

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst (Junior) | Power BI & Excel | Grow Your Career
Description:
Are you a graduate or early-career analyst looking to build your career in data, reporting, and business insights? We are seeking candidates with 1-2 years of relevant experience in data analysis or business intelligence roles.

At Turosi, we're looking for a Junior Business Intelligence Analyst to help transform how we use data across our operations. This is a unique opportunity to step into a growing analytics function where you'll work directly with stakeholders across operations, finance, and supply chain to turn data into real business impact.




About the Role

At Turosi, we’re passionate about delivering excellence across our operations—and we’re looking for a Business Insights Analyst to help us unlock the power of data to drive smarter decisions and continuous improvement.

In this role, you will partner with stakeholders across operations, finance, supply chain, and commercial teams to identify opportunities, solve business challenges, and translate data into meaningful insights that influence performance and strategy.

If you're naturally curious, analytically minded, and enjoy connecting data with real-world outcomes—this is the opportunity for you.




Key Responsibilities

Partner with business units to uncover challenges, inefficiencies, and improvement opportunities

Gather and translate business requirements into actionable reporting and analytical solutions

Develop dashboards, reports, and data models (Power BI, Excel) to support decision-making

Analyse operational and financial performance to identify trends and insights

Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams to deliver impactful outcomes

Support data quality, governance, and reporting improvements

Present clear, actionable insights and recommendations to stakeholders

Drive adoption of data-led decision making across the business




About You

You are a strong problem solver who enjoys working across diverse stakeholders and turning data into insight.

You’ll bring:

Experience in Business Analytics, Reporting, Finance, Operations, or a similar role

Advanced Excel skills (data analysis, modelling, problem solving)

Experience with Power BI (or similar BI tools)

Exposure to SQL or relational databases (desirable)

Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver outcomes

A continuous improvement mindset and attention to detail

Flexible location with opportunities to travel across sites, working closely with stakeholders on real business projects.

Bonus if you have experience in manufacturing, FMCG, or supply chain environments!




At Turosi, we’re more than just a business—we’re a values-driven organisation committed to:

Integrity, trust, and strong relationships

Continuous improvement and innovation

Food safety, sustainability, and animal welfare excellence

Supporting our people, community, and a safe workplace

You’ll be joining a collaborative environment where your insights will directly influence business performance and growth.

Apply Now!

Title: Market Analyst – Energy & Carbon Markets (Graduate)
Description:
About RepuTex

RepuTex is a leading intelligence provider for Australia's energy transition and low-carbon commodities markets, delivering independent pricing, forecasts, market data and technology insights to over 300 customers.

We work across three service streams: Carbon Markets, Energy Transition, and Power & Renewables. At the centre of our service offering is our EnergyIQ platform, used by professionals across Australia's largest traders, industrial emitters, retailers, investors, project developers and financial institutions.

The opportunity

We are seeking a Graduate Market Analyst to join our Melbourne-based research and market data team. This role will support the production of price assessments, market data services, written market commentary and analytical datasets used across our EnergyIQ platform and subscriber research products.

The role is designed for a graduate with a finance, economics or related quantitative background who is interested in learning how commodity and energy markets and policy settings interact across Australia's low-carbon transition.

The successful candidate will work closely with senior analysts to collect, validate and manage market data; support daily price assessment processes; contribute to written reports; and assist with data tasks that support new and existing intelligence products.

This is a strong entry point for candidates seeking exposure to energy, carbon, renewable certificate and low-carbon commodity markets, with structured involvement across both analytical research and practical market data operations.

Key responsibilities

Support RepuTex's price assessment processes across relevant low-carbon commodity and energy transition markets, including collection, validation and organisation of market data inputs.

Lead all market data management, including maintaining trade logs, pricing information, construction of forward curves and engagement with active market participants

Review market submissions, broker information, public datasets and internal data sources to help identify price trends, liquidity conditions and changes in market activity.

Contribute to written market updates, subscriber reports and analytical commentary.

Support data preparation and presentation via the EnergyIQ terminal as well as for client briefings and advisory services.

Use AI tools to improve research, data cleaning, workflow automation, drafting and quality assurance processes, while applying appropriate judgement, verification and confidentiality controls.

Assist with other data tasks required to support new and existing intelligence products, including database maintenance and modelling, public data extraction, tagging, classification and product testing.

Work collaboratively with analysts with product owners across Carbon Markets, Energy Transition and Power & Renewables to ensure market data and research outputs are accurate, timely and internally consistent.

What we’re looking for

We are seeking a graduate or early-career candidate with a strong analytical foundation and a clear interest in market intelligence, data and written research.

A finance, economics, commerce, econometrics, data analytics or related quantitative background.

Strong interest in markets, commodities, energy transition, climate policy, renewable energy or low-carbon investment.

Comfort working with numbers, spreadsheets, datasets and written analysis.

High attention to detail, with the ability to check, reconcile and question data before it is used in market-facing outputs.

Strong written communication skills, including the ability to explain market developments clearly and concisely.

Proficiency with AI tools and a practical interest in applying them to research, data management, automation and productivity workflows.

Working knowledge of Excel or Google Sheets. Exposure to Python, SQL, R, Power BI or similar tools will be highly regarded.

Ability to manage recurring processes and deadlines in a research and data environment.

Applicants must have full and unrestricted working rights in Australia.

Candidates with internship, graduate, research assistant or project experience in finance, economics, energy, commodities, policy, consulting, market research or data analytics are particularly encouraged to apply.

Working with RepuTex

Our team enjoys working in an intellectually engaging environment at the centre of Australia’s energy transition – with a strong focus on substance, curiosity and engaging with the detail of the markets we cover.

Members of our team enjoy a number of benefits:

Flexible leave entitlements

Flexible working hours and hybrid working arrangements

Relaxed and supportive working environment

Fitness centre membership (gym, pool, spa, squash)

End-of-trip facilities (bike storage, lockers, change rooms)

How to apply

Interested candidates should submit both a cover letter and a copy of your CV.

In your cover letter, please briefly outline your interest in markets, energy transition or low-carbon commodities, and describe how you use AI tools in your study, work or personal productivity workflows.

Note: Applicants must have full and unrestricted working rights in Australia.

Title: Graduate- Data & Analytics
Description:
About the role: 
Dive into a career in Water with Unitywater's 2027 Graduate Program alongside five other graduates from a range of disciplines!

Forget sitting on the sidelines - you’ll be contributing from day one..  As soon as you commence as our Data & Analytics Graduate, you'll be doing purpose driven work on real infrastructure that our communities depend on every day. Think six-month rotations across diverse teams, a group innovation project with actual senior backing, and mentoring from people who genuinely want to see you grow.

Two years of structured experience designed to fast-track your career in one of the most essential industries in the country. At the end of the program, you’ll transition into a permanent full-time position. Not bad for your first job out of uni.
As a Data & Analytics Graduate:

Explore how Unitywater's Business Planning and Performance team builds tools, automates processes and turns data into insights that shape real business outcomes.
Learn how we deliver and optimise technology solutions, safeguards data integrity and availability, and manages the performance of technology partnerships across a live utility environment.
Featuring comprehensive training, professional growth opportunities and mentoring and competitive remuneration and benefits
Gain other valuable hands-on experience and exposure across a variety of teams, including business planning & performance, technology and digital solutions, and Change and Transformation. Sharpen your skills and explore where your future in could take you!
This role spans both our Sunshine Coast and Moreton Bay offices, and graduates will be expected to travel between these locations as needed.

We welcome applications from students graduating in 2026 or early 2027, as well as recent graduates within the last two to three years.

 About you: 
Our One Unitywater behaviours - Create the Future, Care Together, and Own It – define how we work together at Unitywater. To contribute to this, you will demonstrate the following skills and qualities for the role:


Open to graduates with a bachelor’s degree in data science, Information Technology or a related field with a major or focus in Data Analytics.
Exposure to coding, whether through platforms, datasets, or just tinkering with tools to automate decisions or solve problems.
Strong written communication, teamwork and collaboration capability.
Demonstrated digital literacy skills, including the ability to use workplace technologies, digital communication tools, and AI tools effectively and responsibly.
Customer focus and a positive and proactive approach to interacting with our community.
Understanding of and commitment to safe behaviours that keep our workplace healthy.
Strong time management, multi-tasking, investigative, analytical and research skills.
Drive to achieve development goals, plans and work across a variety of systems and projects to achieve team outcomes.
High work standards and attention to detail.

Working at Unitywater:

Flexibility: We value flexibility and support work-life balance with hybrid and flexible working, purchased annual leave and access to floating public holidays. Tell us what this looks like for you! 
Wellbeing: We offer several initiatives to support your wellbeing, such as discounted private health insurance, salary packaging, employee assistance program, and access to retailer discounts and benefits. 
Learning and Development: We offer a range of opportunities to feed your curiosity such as: Walk on Country experience, study assistance, and access to a dedicated learning program and professional memberships. 
Diversity: We celebrate, value and include people of all backgrounds, gender and expressions, sexualities, cultures, bodies and abilities. We invite you to join our dedicated employee networks focused on First Nations Reconciliation, LGBTQIA+, CALD, Gender, Life Stage, Disability and Accessibility.
Additional information:

We are proud to be recognised as an employer of choice for women by WORK180
We are a Benchmarcx Top Employer for Candidate Experience 2026 - we commit to respond to every applicant
 
About us: 
Unitywater operates in one of Australia’s most exceptional environments in terms of natural beauty, geographical and demographic breadth. We operate on Kabi Kabi, Jinibara and Turrbal Country, proudly providing safe and reliable water to customers in Moreton Bay, Sunshine Coast and Noosa. We are a water utility serving one of the fastest growing regions in the country. 

We think a pretty good job starts here. 
 
How to apply:   
Please apply online following the links, and make sure to upload your up-to-date resume and cover letter. 

If you’re successful we will need you to undergo a few checks….  We know they might not be the fun part, but you will need to confirm your right to work in Australia and complete relevant pre-employment checks, including verification of criminal history, drivers licence and driving history, alcohol and other drug screening. 

Please note shortlisted applicants will be contacted after the closing date.
 
Applications close:  Midnight Thursday 4th June 2026
 
For further information please contact the Talent Acquisition team at car••••@unitywater.com 

Unitywater respectfully acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the lands and waterways where we live and work -  the Kabi Kabi people, Jinibara people and Turrbal people. We honour their enduring connection to Country and pay our respects to Elders past and present.

Title: 2027 NDIA Graduate Program
Description:
Our participants, Our people, Our purpose

The National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA) is the independent statutory agency responsible for implementing the world-leading National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), supporting eligible Australians with permanent and significant disability. 

After more than a decade of operation, the NDIS is in a major period of change, with a focus on improving participant experience, strengthening integrity, and ensuring long-term financial stability. The NDIA is committed to enhancing the quality of life and increasing both economic and social participation for people with disability.

A new role awaits

It is an exciting time to join the NDIA as we continue to strengthen and reform the Scheme. A key focus for the agency is enhancing service delivery and building capability in allied health to support the NDIS now and into the future.

The NDIA Graduate Program (NDIA GP) is designed to support graduates as they begin their careers in the NDIA and the Australian Public Service (APS). Through a structured learning and development journey, the program combines formal training with meaningful, hands-on experience across a range of business areas within the agency.

The program runs over 12 months and offers a wide range of opportunities to build your skills, broaden your experience, and develop the capabilities needed to thrive in a high-performing organisation. You will gain valuable insight into how the NDIA operates, while making a genuine contribution to important work that supports Australians with disability.

If you are passionate about social inclusion, value diversity, and are looking to kick-start a purposeful and rewarding career, the NDIA GP could be the perfect place to start.

The NDIA Graduate Program gives graduates the opportunity to:
contribute to meaningful work supporting Australians living with disability to have greater participation in the community and the economy
develop the foundational skills, behaviours, mindsets and networks graduates need to make a meaningful contribution to the APS, supported through a formal development framework
gain broad experience and exposure to different business areas through job rotations within the host business area and across the broader NDIA
be part of an inclusive and diverse work environment, where you’ll be mentored to build strong professional networks at the NDIA and in the APS
benefit from a competitive salary and flexible working conditions that support positive work-life balance
access an accelerated career pathway and salary progression from APS4.1 pay point on commencement to APS5.1 pay point upon successful completion of the Graduate Program.
Who we are looking for

The 2027 NDIA Graduate Program is seeking motivated graduates who are passionate about the NDIA’s purpose and committed to making a meaningful impact.

We are looking for individuals who thrive in collaborative environments and enjoy contributing as part of a diverse, high-performing team.

We value diversity in all its forms and recognise that different perspectives strengthen our ability to deliver positive outcomes for Australians with disability.

As the NDIA operates in a complex and fast-changing environment, we are seeking individuals who are adaptable, resilient, and able to respond to evolving priorities while maintaining a strong focus on outcomes.

We strongly encourage applications from people with disability; individuals from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds; people who identify as Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Transgender, Intersex, Queer, Asexual, Sistergirl, Brotherboy Plus (LGBTIQASB+); and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

We welcome applications from graduates with a wide range of academic backgrounds, including (but not limited to):

actuarial
allied health
behavioural science and psychology
business and commerce
computer science, ICT, cyber security, and software development
data and analytics
economics, accounting, and finance
engineering
government, public policy, and political science
human resources
legal
media, marketing, and communications
project and program management
risk
social sciences (including policy, research, health, disability, and social work)

In addition to your academic background, we are seeking graduates who demonstrate:

strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
adaptability and openness to change
data literacy and an ability to work with evidence-based insights
strong collaboration and communication skills
a genuine commitment to the values and purpose of the Australian Public Service (APS)
Eligibility

To be a part of the NDIA Graduate Program you must meet the following criteria:

be an Australian citizen
have completed, or be due to complete by 31 December 2026, at least an Australian Qualifications Framework Level 7 qualification (a bachelor’s degree), or an equivalent or higher qualification
have completed their most recent within the past 5 years at time of application
have and keep a valid Australian Government security clearance upon entry to the program
be willing to undergo police, character, health or other pre-employment checks
Locations

The NDIA operates more than 160 NDIA locations across Australia. Graduates have the opportunity to work from a wide range of these offices, including those in rural and remote areas as well as the following cities, towns and suburbs:

ACT – Canberra
NSW – Newcastle, Sydney, Penrith
NT – Alice Springs, Darwin
QLD – Brisbane, Robina, Townsville
SA – Adelaide
TAS – Hobart
VIC - Richmond, Geelong
WA – Perth

To learn more what it’s like to be an NDIA graduate, please visit the Meet our Graduates page. Further information about the program is also available on the NDIA Graduate Program webpage.

Successful candidates will be required to undergo a National Coordinated Criminal History Check (NCCHC), Worker Screening check and/or Working with Children Check (where required) and provide evidence of Australian Citizenship.

Applications for this role close 01st June 2026 at 11:30pm AEST

For enquiries relating to recruitment, please email: recruitment@ndis.gov.au. For questions specific to the NDIA Graduate Program, please contact GraduateProgram@ndis.gov.au

Please note that, with the exception of personal details, applications can NOT be edited or updated once submitted for consideration. Please make sure you are completely satisfied with your entire application before submitting.

NDIA is committed to supporting an inclusive and diverse workforce and welcomes and encourages applications from People with Disability (PwD), First Nations peoples, LGBTIQA+ people, people from Culturally and Linguistically Diverse backgrounds, Mature age workers, as well as young workers and supporting an inter-generational workforce and supporting gender equity and families. NDIA will provide reasonable adjustments for candidates to participate equitably in the recruitment process and discuss workplace adjustments to fulfil the inherent requirements of the role. If you require an adjustment to be made during the recruitment process (including alternate formats of the application form) or have an enquiry about the support provided, please contact NDIA Recruitment on rec••••••••@ndis.gov.au.

RecruitAbility applies to this position: The RecruitAbility scheme supports people with disability applying for jobs in the Australian Public Service (APS) by giving you a better opportunity to put forward your skills and experience during the selection process.

There are certain roles within the National Disability Insurance Agency that may require mandatory vaccination, subject to government order or directive. Evidence may be required prior to commencement.

Title: Deloitte STEM Connect Virtual Experience
Description:
Studying STEM or interested in a career within the STEM space? Looking to develop your skills with real business problems? Now’s your chance! Our Deloitte STEM Connect Virtual Experience is open for enrolment.

We’re committed to giving you endless opportunities to learn and develop, so that you can thrive in your early career. That’s why we’ve designed a Virtual Experience, accessible 24/7 and open to everyone.

Complete set tasks and unlock sample answers written by our very own Consultants! By solving simulated client cases, you’ll build your experience in STEM and get a glimpse of a day-in-the-life as a Dloitte Consultant. You'll get to experience what it is like to work on a project that mirrors our teams' skills and the work they do in the real world. You will build skills in coding, data analysis, software development, cyber security and forensic technology.

The best part?

You can credit this experience on your CV and LinkedIn profile upon completing our virtual experience program!

Enrol now, and immerse yourself into our world to gain invaluable practical skills, build your confidence, and uncover the exciting opportunities we have for you at Deloitte!
