Title: Graduate Systems Engineer
Description:
About Us:

Crossmuller Systems Integration is a multi-disciplined engineering leader specialising in the design, build, and integration of advanced industrial systems across various customers and industries. Our project-based work spans diverse sectors including Food and Beverage, Data Centres, Manufacturing, Recycling, and Warehouse Automation.

The Role:

As a Graduate Systems Engineer, you will be immersed in the full project lifecycle of cutting-edge industrial systems. You will work alongside our senior engineers to design, develop, test, and commission integrated control solutions that form the backbone of modern industrial facilities. This role is designed to build your technical foundation and develop you into a well-rounded Systems Integration professional.

This is a fantastic opportunity for a passionate graduate to launch their career within a dedicated Systems Integration team, working on a variety of challenging projects from day one.

Key Responsibilities:

Assist in the design and development of industrial control systems using PLCs, SCADA, HMI, and MES platforms

Support the configuration of network infrastructure for industrial applications (Ethernet/IP, Profinet, MODBUS, BACNET etc.)

Participate in software testing at our headquarters, and on-site commissioning at client facilities

Collaborate with Project and Technical leads to deliver solutions

Contribute to project documentation, including functional design specifications, test plans, and user manuals

Gain exposure to a wide range of technologies, including industrial communication protocols, PLC languages, SCADA, MES, and Database systems

About You:

You are a recent graduate (or in your final year) of a degree in Software, Mechatronic, Electrical, or Computer Systems Engineering

You have a fundamental understanding of software development and control system concepts

You are curious, proactive, and have a strong desire to learn new technologies and solve complex problems

You possess excellent communication skills and enjoy working as part of a collaborative team

Skills and Attributes:

A foundational knowledge of programming (e.g., Structured Text, Ladder Logic, Python, C#, or similar). Specific platforms are less important than core programming concepts

Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications

Excellent problem-solving and analytical abilities

Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high-quality work

Excellent time management and the ability to prioritise tasks effectively

Eligibility Requirements:

A degree (or near completion) in a relevant engineering field

Available to work full-time

Possess a valid driver's licence

Title: Lead Data Scientist
Description:
Lead Data Scientist

We are hiring a Lead Data Scientist to own and build the data science function from the ground up. This is a hands-on, high-impact role focused on turning complex product, customer and operational data into measurable business value.
This is not a strategy-only position. You will design the approach, build the models, engineer pipelines, create insights, and establish the data practices that support a growing AI-powered platform. You will work closely with engineering, product, customer success, operations, finance and leadership to define what data means across the business and how it can drive better decisions.
About the role As the first dedicated data science hire, you will have the opportunity to shape the company’s data strategy, build the foundations of a modern data stack, and create the models, dashboards and reporting frameworks that teams rely on.
You will work across machine learning, applied AI, data engineering, analytics, governance and business intelligence. The role is suited to someone who is highly technical, commercially minded and comfortable operating in a fast-moving environment where they can build from scratch.

Key responsibilities
Design, build and deploy machine learning models and statistical analyses that solve real business problems
Work hands-on with LLM and RAG system data, including retrieval quality, conversation outcomes, latency and model performance
Build experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing and causal inference, to validate product and business decisions
Develop NLP and text analytics capability to extract insights from conversation data at scale
Architect scalable data pipelines across cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP or Azure
Build and maintain a modern data stack across ingestion, transformation, warehousing, orchestration and data quality
Establish data governance practices including cataloguing, lineage, access controls and compliance frameworks
Create dashboards, scorecards and executive reporting across product, engineering, customer success, finance, operations and leadership
Define key metrics across customer experience, product reliability, unit economics and operational health
Translate ambiguous business questions into clear, measurable data problems
Build the business case for growing the data function and hire or mentor future team members as needed
What you will bring
8–10+ years’ experience across data science, data engineering, analytics or applied AI/ML
Proven experience building and deploying machine learning models in production
Strong Python and SQL capability
Experience with modern data tooling such as dbt, Airflow, Dagster, Spark, Snowflake, BigQuery or Redshift
Experience architecting cloud-native data platforms across AWS, GCP or Azure
Hands-on experience with LLM-based systems, RAG architectures, vector databases and orchestration frameworks
Strong statistical foundations across experimental design, causal inference, regression, classification and time-series analysis
Experience with data governance, privacy and compliance in enterprise or regulated environments
Ability to communicate complex data insights to senior stakeholders and non-technical audiences
Experience in SaaS, AI/ML product companies or enterprise implementation environments
Nice to have
Experience working in the Australian market and familiarity with Australian privacy legislation
Familiarity with conversational AI metrics such as deflection, containment, intent accuracy and ASR/TTS performance
MLOps experience across model versioning, monitoring and CI/CD for ML pipelines
A quantitative degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, physics, engineering or similar
Cloud or data certifications such as AWS Solutions Architect or GCP Professional Data Engineer
What success looks like In the first 30 days, you will complete a deep immersion into the product, data landscape and AI architecture, assess the current state of data, and deliver a clear roadmap covering risks, quick wins, metrics and priorities.
Over the following months, you will strengthen core data platform components, launch operational scorecards, improve data quality and model performance, and establish reporting rhythms across the business.
Over 6–12 months, you will have built a trusted data foundation, improved business outcomes through data science, matured metric ownership, and developed a clear plan for growing the data function.
Benefits
Join a fast-growing, innovative technology environment
Build and lead a data function from the ground up
Work at the forefront of AI, data science and conversational technology
High level of ownership, autonomy and business impact
Strong career growth and professional development opportunities
Competitive salary
Flexible, remote-first working environment

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About the role

As a Data Engineer, you’ll bring a well-rounded understanding of data-driven practices to support senior team members and stakeholders in enhancing regulatory processes. From uncovering insights through data profiling and analytics to building robust models and engineering solutions, you’ll play a key role in how data is governed, interpreted, and transformed into actionable intelligence.

Key responsibilities of this role are:

Participate and collaborate in a high performing SCRUM team to deliver on key BAU activities and milestones from the Project Roadmap, and Data Science and Analytics Strategy.
Demonstrate excellent judgement skills at interpreting business requirements, estimating workloads, prioritizing tasks, and managing time in the delivery of quality solutions to meet deadlines.
Develop highly performant, efficiently administered, cost considerate, and fully secure data pipeline solutions.
Support, maintain, and upgrade existing data workflows, deployment pipelines, and infrastructure environments through all stages of application life cycle management.
Participate in rotating incident management duties which includes troubleshooting and resolving incidents, performing RCAs, configuring monitoring, and writing troubleshooting guides.
Operate using modern CI/CD methods with automated testing, code reviews, and CAB approved releases.
Proactively remediate and document operational incidents, project risks, and impediments where possible or otherwise surface these items as appropriate to project managers and senior team members.

NHVR’s Data & Technology teams are currently undertaking growth and considerable transformation, and this is an exciting time to join the organisation.

To learn more, the position description can be found on the NHVR Careers site - https://careers.nhvr.gov.au/jobs/search

 

About You

To be successful in this role you will have a minimum of four years Data Engineering experience with proven skills using analytic software, ideally possess a bachelor’s degree in relevant field and be experienced with Agile Scrum, SDLC and data lifecycle processed.

Your toolkit for success:

Demonstrated experience with ETL/ELT packages, pipelines, or integrations.
Proven skills with RMDBS systems and concepts including normalization, data modelling, schemas, etc.
Proven skills with object-oriented programming, design patterns, unit testing, CI/CD, and debugging.
Demonstrated knowledge of Microsoft BI Stack, SQL Server, Integration Services, Analysis Services, Azure Data Factory, Databricks, DevOps.
Demonstrated knowledge of Java, Python, R, Power Query M, or DAX languages.
Demonstrated knowledge of Big Data concepts: structure, unstructured, streaming, data lake, governance, lineage, etc.

Ideally you will have:

Accredited Professional or Associate Databricks Data Engineer.
Accredited Azure Certification.
Knowledge of data visualization tools such as Microsoft PowerBI, Tableau.
Knowledge of machine learning tools, platforms, and processes.

 

Why join us?

The National Heavy Vehicle Regulator (NHVR) commenced operations in 2013 as the national entity responsible for regulating all vehicles in Australia over 4.5 tonnes gross vehicle mass. The NHVR minimises the compliance burden on the heavy vehicle transport industry, reduces duplication and inconsistences across state and territory borders, and ultimately for the heavy vehicle business with government in Australia.

We are headquartered in Brisbane and employ more than 1000 people across the ACT, New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania and Victoria.

We offer-

Salary package of $109,640.95 + superannuation
Monthly RDO and paid leave over Christmas closure.
Hybrid working environment – up to 2 days from home.
Up to15.25% superannuation through a co-contribution scheme
Leave loading 17.5%
Wellbeing programs – discounted fitness through Fitness Passport, Discount health insurance, flu vaccine, financial programs including discounted banking products, finance information sessions.
Salary packaging options including novated lease.
Discounted IT products including Microsoft and free family password security plan.
Ongoing training and professional development support
Professional membership 

Application Process

Please apply with your CV and cover letter addressing the essential requirements of the role.

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to contact the NHVR recruitment team on recruitment@nhvr.gov.au.

Successful applicants will be required to complete standard pre-employment checks including a national police and work rights check.

Please Note: We will not require any support from Recruitment Agencies at this time and kindly request no contact is made. Only direct applications will be considered.

We are committed to being an inclusive, diverse and flexible workplace where differences are valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experience and abilities.

Title: AI Solutions Developer
Description:
About Us

We are one of the largest and fastest growing medical technology companies in the world. Our local team is leading the field of digital dentistry and improving outcomes significantly for doctors and patients alike.

Our commitment to advancing dental care through cutting-edge digital solutions drives our continuous investment in technology and operational excellence.

Our success is built on the drive and commitment of our people. We will recognise your talent and your skills and we will reward you for them in a fun and inclusive working environment.

About the Role

We are looking for an AI Solutions Developer to join our tech team. You will play a core role in designing and building generative AI solutions that enhance how our business operates and how we deliver value to doctors and patients. This position plays a key role in our digital transformation initiatives and is central to how we bring emerging AI capabilities into real, production-ready applications.

You'll combine solid software engineering fundamentals with deep curiosity about the rapidly evolving generative AI space — turning new models, tools, and techniques into practical solutions that make a measurable difference.

What You'll Do

Design, build, and deploy generative AI applications and features that solve real business problems

Develop solutions using LLMs, embeddings, vector databases, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines

Integrate AI capabilities with existing business systems, APIs, and data sources

Evaluate emerging models, frameworks, and tools — and help guide decisions on what's worth adopting

Build evaluation frameworks, guardrails, and monitoring to ensure AI features are reliable, safe, and useful

Collaborate with stakeholders across the business to understand requirements and translate them into AI-powered solutions

Write clean, tested, maintainable code and contribute to engineering standards across the team

Create documentation and share knowledge with technical and non-technical colleagues

Support and continuously improve deployed solutions through troubleshooting and iteration

Technical Requirements

Core Skills (Essential)

3–5 years of professional software development experience with strong fundamentals in data structures, algorithms, system design, and writing production-quality code

Proficiency in Python and/or TypeScript

Hands-on experience building with LLMs — including prompt engineering, RAG, agents, or fine-tuning

Familiarity with the modern GenAI ecosystem (e.g. LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector stores like Pinecone or pgvector, providers such as Anthropic, OpenAI, or open-source models)

Experience integrating REST APIs and web services

Cloud platform experience

SQL knowledge and database concepts

Git or similar version control experience

Education and Work Rights

Bachelor's Degree or higher in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience)

Full working rights in Australia (citizenship, permanent residency, etc.) - Required

Professional Qualities

Effective communication with both technical and non-technical team members

Self-directed and capable of managing multiple projects

Strong commitment to staying current with the generative AI landscape — you read the papers, follow releases, try new tools, and have informed opinions on what's hype and what's real

Additional Qualifications We Value

Experience with MLOps or LLMOps tooling

Background in AI evaluation, observability, or safety

Exposure to multi-modal models or agentic systems

Contributions to open-source AI projects

Experience deploying AI solutions in regulated industries (healthcare, medical devices, etc.)

What We Offer

Competitive salary package

Opportunity to work at the cutting edge of generative AI in a real-world, high-impact setting

Direct impact on business operations, digital transformation, and patient outcomes

Application Requirements

Please submit:

Current CV/Resume

Cover letter highlighting relevant generative AI and software development experience

Portfolio or GitHub repository showcasing relevant AI/ML or software projects

Title: Graduate Firmware Engineer
Description:
About Us

We're on a mission to accelerate the world's transition to electric mobility, and we're looking for a curious, driven graduate to join the team building our next generation of DC fast chargers.

Tritium's firmware team is small and high-performing — which means you'll ship real work quickly, sit alongside experienced engineers, and see your code running in the wild, at EV charging stations across the globe.

Why Tritium

Work on technology that genuinely matters — accelerating EV adoption globally
Join a tight-knit engineering team in Brisbane where your contributions are visible
Contribute to products deployed in real-world charging networks worldwide
A culture built on integrity, innovation, and excellence
Half day off each month to invest in yourself
Free onsite charging for your EV!

Title: Finance Graduate Program Mid - 2027 Intake
Description:
The Organisation

The Australian Security Intelligence Organisation (ASIO) protects Australia and Australians from threats to their security. ASIO’s people are ordinary Australians but they do extraordinary things – they are our most important asset. To be successful, we need talented people who are highly capable, dedicated, adaptable and resilient.

We seek to reflect the community we protect. ASIO is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment, where all staff are valued and respected. We welcome applications from all eligible candidates, irrespective of gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religious affiliation, age or disability. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples are encouraged to apply. We are secretive about what we do, not what we value.

At a time when Australia’s security environment is dynamic, diverse and degrading, our work is more important than ever. Join the mission and make a difference every day.

The opportunity

Are you seeking an exceptional career where you can contribute and make a difference to the security of Australia?

The ASIO Finance Graduate Program is a dynamic 12-month program with intakes scheduled for mid-2027. The program aims to provide future professionals with the skills, knowledge and attributes required to support public financial management, strategic investment strategies and digital enablement. Our graduates are curious, conscientious and results-driven.

We are seeking candidates who are looking for meaningful careers in finance and accounting, with an aspiration to professionalise with Chartered Accountants Australia and New Zealand or CPA Australia.

Over the 12-month program, the Organisation will guide and mentor you to further develop your skills and knowledge to contribute to ASIO’s mission. You will undertake placements across a number of finance and accounting areas, giving you opportunities to work alongside colleagues throughout the Organisation.

Fields of study we are looking for include, but are not limited to:

• Finance

• Commerce

• Accounting

• Business

On successful completion of the program, you will be promoted to the AE5 level (APS5 equivalent).  

A merit pool may be created to fill future vacancies which has the same or similar requirements as this position. This merit pool will be valid for up to 18 months.

What you will bring

We are seeking candidates with the following attributes:

• Adaptability

• Conscientious

• Curiosity

• Problem solving

• Resilience

• Results-driven

We are seeking candidates with the following capabilities:

• Ability to be coached and mentored to develop your professional judgement.

• Ability to operate independently and in teams.

• Ability to build productive relationships with internal and external stakeholders.

• Demonstrate good interpersonal and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to demonstrate a client-focus.

• Have the potential to provide business advice and problem solve.

• Personal qualities that exemplify ASIO’s values and expected behaviours.

Qualifications

To apply for a graduate program in ASIO, you must have completed a relevant degree prior to program commencement, or no more than 3 years prior.

What we offer you

ASIO provides a number of benefits to its staff including:

• A competitive salary, including a 7.5% allowance for maintaining a TOP SECRET-Privileged Access security clearance.

• Employer superannuation contributions of 15.4%.

• A variety of leave options in addition to the standard 4 weeks annual leave, including stand down between Christmas and New Year.

• Flexible working arrangements. Please note due to our unique working environment, work from home options are not available.

• Study assistance, including financial support and study leave for tertiary education.

• Access to 7 staff-led diversity and inclusion networks.

• Access to an Employee Assistance Program (EAP).

• Mentoring opportunities.

Eligibility

To be eligible to work at ASIO, you must be:

• an Australian citizen.

• have completed your degree before the program commences, or graduated in the past 3 years.

• assessed as suitable to hold and maintain a TOP SECRET-Privileged Access (TS-PA) security clearance.

Reasonable adjustments

Please let us know if you require any additional assistance or reasonable adjustments during any stage of the recruitment process in order to fully participate in the recruitment process or the workplace. This may include reasonable adjustments to assessment methodologies to enable full participation.

How to apply

ASIO officers’ identities are protected by law. Under section 92 of the ASIO Act, the only declared ASIO officers are the Director-General and Deputy Directors-General. Due to the need to protect our people’s identities, ASIO holds all employment applications in the strictest of confidence. It is essential that you do the same. Please do not discuss your application with others as doing so may adversely affect your potential employment.

Click on ‘Apply online’ to commence your application. Your application must be complete and include the following:

• A written pitch of up to 500 words using examples to demonstrate how your skills and experience meet the requirements of the role.

• A current CV, no more than 2 pages in length, outlining your employment history, the dates and a brief description of your role, as well as any academic qualifications or relevant training you may have undertaken.

• A copy of your academic transcript. If you have not yet graduated, please provide your most up-to-date transcript. This can be an unofficial copy as long as it is issued by your tertiary provider.

• Details of 2 referees, which must include a current or previous supervisor. ASIO will not contact your referees without advising you prior to doing so.

Read the ASIO People Capability Framework and Core Capability Guide, available on ASIO’s website. Ensure your responses address the relevant capabilities and levels outlined in these frameworks.

Use of AI in recruitment processes

ASIO does not use any form of artificial intelligence at any point in the recruitment process.

All applications are reviewed by a selection panel. We recognise some applicants may use AI technologies to draft applications or prepare for interviews. If you wish to use AI, we recommend doing this only for initial drafts and ensure that your application is personalised and accurately reflects your skills, experience and qualifications.

Additionally, be aware of the privacy policies of any AI tool you use. Be mindful of the information you are entering into AI tools (personal details, work history, etc) and how using it may link you to potential ASIO employment, noting that you are expected to be discrete and maintain confidentiality with your application.

If you are invited to attend interview, the use of AI tools and recording devices during interview is strictly prohibited. Should AI tools or recording services be detected, your application, and any future applications may be discontinued.

The recruitment selection process – timeframes

Our selection process is rigorous and extensive. All employment decisions and selection processes at ASIO are based on merit principles and candidates must be prepared to undergo various selection stages. The stages may include application assessment, eligibility checks, online testing, skills-based assessment and interview.

We ask all applicants for their patience throughout the process.

As we are recruiting for the Finance Graduate Program mid-2027 intake, candidates can expect the following timeframes for the recruitment selection process:

• Program advertised in May and June 2026.

• Assessment centres will likely run in November 2026.

Employment conditions

Employment is under the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation Act 1979. Conditions of service are similar to those applying in the Australian Public Service. We recognise entitlements accrued under the Public Service Act 1999 and provide for continuation of superannuation under the Commonwealth schemes. Salary packaging arrangements are also available.

Australian Workplace Equality Index

ASIO has achieved the Australian Workplace Equality Index (AWEI) Gold Standard in 2024 and 2025, recognising ASIO’s inclusion work and positive culture. ASIO was the first Australian intelligence agency to achieve this status.

For more information about ASIO, please visit: www.asio.gov.au.

Title: Statistician and Data Scientist - Gordonvale
Description:
About Sugar Research Australia (SRA)
Sugar Research Australia invests in and manages a portfolio of research, development and adoption projects that drive productivity, profitability and sustainability for the Australian sugarcane industry.
We have highly dedicated and passionate people working on industry-critical projects to deliver tangible value outcomes and impact for our industry stakeholders.

About the Role
The Statistician and Data Scientist provide quantitative and analytical support to the Variety Development program through the application of advanced statistical methods and data science techniques. The role focuses on delivering high-quality analyses of breeding trials, genomic data, and research experiments to inform selection decisions. It also contributes to the development of new analytical approaches and reporting tools.

Key Responsibilities Include:

Lead and deliver statistical analyses of plant breeding selection trials to provide reliable insights on candidate clones and key trial metrics to support variety selection decisions.
Analyse annual plant breeding disease screening trials and produce actionable outputs on trial performance and disease resistance metrics.
Develop and manage analytical pipelines to support genomic selection, GWAS, and other quantitative genetics analyses.
Provide support to the integration of high-throughput phenotypic data into routine analysis pipelines.
Provide statistical and scripting support to genomics laboratory activities, including DNA identity testing workflows.
Develop and deliver automated, reproducible reports and dashboards (Shiny, Dash) for analysis outputs and key reporting metrics.
Coordinate with Variety Officers and Pathology staff to ensure timely data upload, analysis, and reporting aligned with operational deadlines.
Collaborate on plant breeding research projects, providing advanced statistical expertise and analytical input.
About You
You will bring with you the following qualifications, capabilities and experience:
  
Essential

Tertiary qualification in statistics, quantitative genetics, data science, or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience in advanced statistical analysis, including mixed models, multi-environment trial analysis, spatial modelling, and other quantitative genetics methods.
Strong capability in analysing high-dimensional or large-scale datasets using machine learning, and other advanced analytical methods.
Demonstrated advanced proficiency in data analysis programming languages such as R, SAS, or Python.
Experience using modern data reporting and visualisation tools, such as Shiny or Dash.
Proficiency in SQL and database systems for data extraction and management.
Desirable

Experience with genomic analysis methods such as GWAS or genomic selection.
Postgraduate qualification (Masters or PhD) in a relevant field such as quantitative genetics, biometrics, or computational biology.
To Apply
Please note this is a permanent, ongoing role and some intra- and inter-state travel may be required.
  
For further information, please refer to the Position Description https://sugarresearch.com.au/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/2026-Position-Description-Statistician-and-Data-Scientist.pdf

If your capabilities and experience meet the requirements of the role and you would like to be part of our dedicated team, please click ‘Apply’ to submit your application by 17 June 2026. Your application should include your resume and address the requirements of the position.

Applicants must have the right to work in Australia in order to apply. The successful applicant will also be required to undertake medical screening, drug and alcohol screening, and a police check.

Title: Graduate Data Science and Analytics
Description:
Job description:

About the role Join a 2026 AFR BOSS Best Places to Work Gold Winner recognised by the Australian Financial Review (AFR) and kickstart your career with InfraBuild, proudly recognised as a 2026 Sustainability Champion by the World Steel Association We would like to invite recent or soon-to-be graduates to take part in our 2027 Graduate Program within the Data Science and Analytics discipline based in Chipping Norton. Real Work with Real Outcomes - Starting in January 2027, our program offers a two-year fixed-term contract with the potential for permanent placement. This immersive, hands-on program provides practical learning through hands-on experience and meaningful work.
Participants will complete two 12-month rotational placements, gaining a comprehensive technical and commercial skills training blend.
The program includes continuous coaching, mentorship, and formal training, allowing participants to rotate through diverse business units.
This structure ensures a thorough understanding of operations and strategic initiatives, combining on-the-job learning with formal education.
Key Areas of Exposure
Analyse large datasets from production, supply chain, and quality control to uncover insights and trends.
Create data-driven strategies to reduce costs, enhance product quality, and increase productivity.
Enable informed decision-making to streamline processes and drive sustainable growth for the company.
Analyse market trends and economic indicators to forecast steel demand and price fluctuations, aiding in strategic planning and budgeting

We are seeking adaptable, motivated individuals who demonstrate:
A strong foundation in Data Science and Analytics
Strong problem-solving and communication skills
Curiosity, initiative and a willingness to learn
A proactive approach to learning, teamwork, and safety
A commitment to driving continuous improvement and making meaningful contributions to the business.
Please upload your academic transcript in the cover letter section of your application. Eligibility Criteria:
A genuine passion for Data Science and Analytics within the operational context of steel manufacturing and distribution.
Australian/NZ citizenship or a visa granting unlimited working rights.
Completion of an Australian tertiary degree within the past three years (or set to conclude in 2026).
Less than two years of full-time, discipline-specific work experience.
Note: Successful placement into this program will be subject to pre-employment checks, including but not limited to criminal, qualification, medical, and reference checks. Please upload your academic transcript in the cover letter section of your application.

Employee Benefits We offer a range of benefits designed to support you at work and beyond:
Paid parental leave that goes further - 20 weeks paid Primary Carer Leave and 3 weeks Partner Leave, plus superannuation contributions during unpaid leave (eligibility applies)
Health cover at discounted rates - Access corporate rates with nib Health Insurance to support you and your family
Everyday savings - Access a wide range of employee discounts across major brands including retail, travel and lifestyle
Flexible vehicle options - Novated leasing options available, providing a tax-effective way to finance and manage your vehicle
Support when you need it - Confidential counselling and wellbeing support through our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Sonder, available to you and your immediate family
Why Work Here? We offer more than just a job; we provide a dynamic environment built on three pillars that define our commitment to both our team and our industry.
Work with a greater purpose - join us on our pursuit of a sustainable future and contribute to our nation's legacy.
Work well, live better - experience a safe, supportive and inclusive environment which values you as a whole person and where wellbeing matters.
Build their own future - grow and drive your own development to grow your skills. Every day is an opportunity to grow.
We invite you to forge your career in a workplace where passion meets purpose, wellbeing is nurtured and the opportunity to thrive is in your hands. About Us InfraBuild Recycling collects and processes scrap to supply our steelmaking operations and global markets. With a national network of sites, we play a critical role in Australia's circular steel system, turning scrap into new steel and supporting more efficient, lower embodied carbon construction. Inclusion We're committed to building an inclusive workplace that reflects the communities we serve. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. Your diversity helps us create more sustainable and innovative steel solutions for Australia's future. We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we commit to respond to every applicant.

Title: Specialist Data Science (12-month FTC)
Description:
Company description:



SPECIALIST DATA SCIENCE (12-MONTH FTC)



Job description:



DISCOVER A BRIGHTER FUTURE

At South32, we're meeting the challenge of enabling a sustainable future through our focus on responsibly producing commodities critical for a low-carbon world.

As a collaborative, caring and inclusive company, there's no better place to make a genuine difference. Together we're making a positive, global impact that has the potential to change lives.

Whatever career path you choose, we'll support you every step of the way to learn, grow and succeed. So join us, step towards a brighter future and leave your legacy.

ABOUT PERTH OFFICE

South32's Perth office is based in the heart of the CBD and is a modern, for-purpose workplace designed to foster collaboration, innovation and performance. The office houses key functions including Human Resources, Finance, Investor Relations, Corporate Development, Commercial, Technical, Legal and External Affairs. Our office is conveniently located a short five-minute walk from the Perth Underground and Elizabeth Quay Train Stations.

WHAT SOUTH32 OFFERS

Annual short-term incentive bonus that recognises both your individual performance and the overall success of the business
Superannuation Contributions at 14%
Ability to purchase up to 4 weeks of additional annual leave
The opportunity to become a shareholder of South32 through our share plans
Flexible work options include working from home, flexible hours & job sharing
26 weeks full-paid or 52 weeks of half-paid parental leave for primary carers

ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a 12-month fixed term, full-time position working Monday to Friday. The ideal candidate will preferably be based at our head office in Perth, but other locations may be considered.

The Specialist, Data Science will assist with building practical AI solutions that make a real difference in mining operations. In this role, you'll work on developing and implementing machine learning and generative AI tools that improve safety outcomes, boost operational efficiency, and drive better financial results across our sites.

You'll be part of a team focused on turning data into actionable insights, creating solutions for our operations teams, and ensuring these technologies deliver measurable improvements where they matter most.

Reporting to the Lead Data Science, other key responsibilities include, but aren't limited to:

Build and implement AI solutions that solve real problems in our mining operations, focusing on practical applications that improve safety, reduce costs, and increase productivity
Work on data science projects from concept to deployment, collaborating closely with business teams to ensure what you build meets their needs and can be scaled across operations
Guide and support colleagues in data science approaches and methods, helping teams across the organisation make better decisions using data
Help grow our data science capabilities by sharing insights, establishing good practices, and working with different teams to show how data can drive better outcomes

ABOUT YOU

You will have experience and demonstrate capability in the following:

Solid data science foundation with hands-on experience in machine learning, computer vision, and generative AI - you know how to work with data and build models that work
Technical skills in Python and SQL - these are your main tools for getting things done, and you're comfortable using them daily
Experience deploying models in production environments using Docker and other deployment tools - you've taken models from development to real-world use
Ability to create clear, useful visualisations with Power BI or similar platforms that help people understand what the data is telling them
Familiarity with modern development practices, including Git version control, MLOps workflows, and pipeline tools like MLFlow or Databricks
Effective communication skills - you can explain technical concepts to different audiences and work effectively with both technical teams and business stakeholders

Qualifications for this position are:

Relevant tertiary qualification such as a Bachelor's or Master's degree in STEM (Mathematics / Statistics / Computer Science preferred)

South32 is committed to building and maintaining an inclusive and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate. We provide a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

This job advert will close on 29 May 2026

Title: Data Scientist – Energy Markets
Description:
Role & Company Overview

Role Overview
We’re looking for a highly analytical and numbers drive Data Scientist to join our Melbourne trading floor and contribute directly to the models that drive commercial decisions across our battery, wind, and solar portfolio.
You’ll build and maintain forecasting models, work with real NEM market data daily, and apply AI and machine learning tools as a core part of how you work. This role suits someone with a strong forecasting foundation who is eager to deepen their expertise in energy markets, working alongside experienced traders.
You’ll be on-site in our Brighton office, embedded in the daily rhythm of the trading team, close to the action, with direct visibility of how your work translates into commercial outcomes.
A core part of this opportunity is working daily alongside some of Australia’s most talented energy traders, helping to develop the trading tools and strategies that directly improve trading performance and revenues. Much of this work happens in real time, meaning your contributions will have an immediate and measurable impact on the desk’s commercial outcomes.

About the Company
Equis through one of its subsidiary companies is one of Australia’s leading renewable energy developers, owning and operating one of the nation’s largest battery energy storage system (BESS) portfolios. 
Equis is driving Australia’s transition to a clean energy future through an ambitious portfolio of large-scale battery, and wind assets across the NEM.
Our flagship asset is the Melbourne Renewable Energy Hub (MREH) in Plumpton, Victoria, one of the largest battery projects in the world, delivering 600 MW / 1,600 MWh of storage, co-owned with SEC Victoria. Beyond MREH, Equis is developing a national BESS portfolio spanning New South Wales, South Australia, and Queensland, alongside an onshore wind portfolio across Queensland, Tasmania, and New South Wales. Our portfolio has expanded at an average of 174 MW per month since late 2021, and we have operations and teams across Australia, Singapore, Japan, and South Korea.

Job Requirements

Skills & Experience

Essential
Strong experience in data science, quantitative analysis, or a closely related field
Strong understanding of time-series analysis, statistical modelling, and model evaluation methods
Proven track record building forecasting or predictive models and deploying them in production or semi-production environments
Strong Python skills across the data science stack: pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, and at least one deep learning framework (PyTorch or TensorFlow)
Proficient use of AI tools (Claude or similar)
Proficiency in SQL and experience working with cloud data platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Desirable
Experience working with electricity market data, AEMO NEM data (dispatch prices, FCAS, generation, bids/offers) is a significant advantage
Familiarity with energy trading concepts: spot markets, ancillary services, battery dispatch, renewable generation
Exposure to weather-driven forecasting and Numerical Weather Prediction data integration
Experience with MLOps practices: model versioning, monitoring, and automated pipeline deployment
Education
Bachelor's degree or higher in a quantitative discipline: Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, or Economics
Postgraduate qualification in data science or machine learning is a plus but not required

Key Competencies

Analytical rigour, methodical approach to model development, testing, and validation
Commercial awareness, ability to connect modelling work to trading outcomes and revenue impact
Curiosity and initiative, proactively seeks out new techniques, data sources, and market insights
Communication, able to explain complex models clearly to traders and non-technical stakeholders
Teamwork, thrives in a collaborative, office-based team environment


Key responsibilities

Analytics, Forecasting & Modelling
Analyse and interrogate NEM behaviour and asset performance to investigate potential trading opportunities
Develop short-term electricity price forecasting and generation forecasting models for the NEM
Support battery dispatch optimisation modelling, incorporating energy arbitrage and FCAS revenue streams
Back-test and validate model performance against live market outcomes and contribute to ongoing model improvement
AI & Machine Learning
Apply ML techniques and AI more generally to enhance analytics, forecasting and modelling responsibilities
Use AI-assisted tools to accelerate analysis and reporting
Stay current with advances in forecasting and AI/ML and bring relevant new techniques to the team
Data & Pipelines 
Work with data pipelines ingesting AEMO market data, SCADA telemetry, and weather feeds
Ensure model outputs are accurate, reliable, and delivered to trading systems in a timely manner
Maintain data quality standards and flag anomalies or market events that affect model inputs
Collaboration
Work closely with traders on the floor to translate commercial questions into modelling problems
Clearly communicate model outputs, assumptions, and limitations to both technical and non-technical colleagues
Contribute to team knowledge-sharing, code reviews, and documentation


What We Offer


Competitive base salary plus performance bonus
Full-time office role in our Brighton office, you’ll be embedded in the action
Direct mentorship from experienced energy traders and exposure to live trading decisions
Strong learning opportunities and environment, exposure to live NEM trading across multiple jurisdictions
Modern cloud-native data stack, best-in-class tooling, no legacy systems
Dedicated learning & development budget and support for industry certifications and conferences
A genuine opportunity to grow into a senior role as the team and portfolio expand

Title: Quantitative Developer (Python) - Consulting
Description:
Our Data Intelligence Team

Clayton Utz is one of Australia's leading law firms, acting for a diverse range of domestic and global private sector and government clients.

Our client-facing Data Intelligence practice is at the forefront of innovation, recognised as one of the country's fastest-growing forensic, cyber, and data analytics consulting practices.

As an integral part of our broader Advisory, Risk & Regulation stream, our Data Intelligence practice collaborates seamlessly with our legal practice groups to deliver a full suite of forensic technology services to our clients. From forensic accounting and investigations to data analytics, cyber security, and eDiscovery, we provide impactful solutions that make a real difference for our clients.

The Opportunity

We're seeking an experienced quantitative developer with advanced Python and SQL skills to join our collaborative and high-performing Data Intelligence practice in Sydney. This is a unique, client-facing role where your expertise in advanced quantitative data analytics and visualisation technology will contribute to delivering tailored, bespoke solutions to highly sophisticated clients across a range of sectors, including Government, Financial Services, Education and Consumer.

In this role, you will collaborate with lawyers and clients alike to transform data into reliable evidence, critical to achieving positive client outcomes for forensic investigations, remediations, regulatory compliance and major litigious disputes.

Key Responsibilities

In this role, you'll have the opportunity to:

Develop and refine analytical scripts in SQL and Python to address complex, real-world challenges.
Collaborate with diverse stakeholders to design bespoke data analytics solutions that drive results.
Work with data from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy and integrity through validation and transformation.
Create innovative scripts using API integrations with cutting-edge tools like Relativity.
Contribute to a culture of excellence by reviewing and refining code, sharing knowledge, and mentoring others.
Present your work confidently in clear, engaging ways that resonate with clients and colleagues alike.
Partner with cross-functional teams, including lawyers and other specialists, to deliver holistic solutions.



What We're Looking For

We value diverse perspectives and experiences. To thrive in this role, you'll bring:

3+ years of experience in quantitative development or data analytics, gained within a professional or financial services environment.
A strong academic background in fields such as Computer Science, Actuarial Studies, Statistics, Mathematics, or Physics.
Advanced proficiency in both SQL and Python, with a passion for solving complex problems through data.
A meticulous approach to ensuring accuracy and quality in your work.
Strong project management skills and the ability to manage multiple client engagements simultaneously.
Outstanding communication skills, enabling you to build strong relationships and positively influence clients and colleagues.
A collaborative mindset and the ability to adapt to a multidisciplinary environment.



Why Join Clayton Utz?

At Clayton Utz, we're more than just a law firm - we're a community that values our people and their contributions.

When you join us, you'll enjoy:

Access to professional development programs and career growth opportunities.
The tools to perform your role to your best ability, with world-class infrastructure and resources including leading AI platform, Harvey.
A focus on health and wellbeing, with initiatives to support your physical and mental health.
A collaborative and inclusive culture that celebrates diversity and fosters belonging.
Up to 26 weeks' paid parental leave, with flexibility over two years, plus paid superannuation.
The option to purchase up to 4 weeks of additional leave annually.

Title: Postdoctoral Fellow
Description:
Reference number: 26122a
Term: Fixed-term, full-time basis for 3 years
Remuneration: $96,342 to $130,727 pa (LEVA) (inclusive of $81,410 to $110,466 salary pa, 17% superannuation and 17.5% recreation leave loading) OR $137,608 to $163,427 pa (LEVB) (inclusive of $116,280 to $138,097 salary pa, 17% superannuation and 17.5% recreation leave loading)
Location: Gardens Point
Open to: Applicants who have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of the fixed-term appointment. In support of our strategic priority of Indigenous Australian success, Aboriginal Australians and Torres Strait Islander people are encouraged to apply.

Who are we looking for?


We are seeking a Postdoctoral Fellow to join School of Electrical Engineering and Robotics, Faculty of Engineering.

We are looking for a creative and enthusiastic Postdoctoral Fellow to contribute positively with novel Machine Learning to our Biomedical Robotics research program. You should be excited to do pioneering research in Machine Learning for Robotic Scene Understanding in deformable domains. You are driven to investigate how autonomous agents can understand and represent the geometry, semantics, and functionality of their surroundings and the task-relevant objects therein; especially in dynamic and uncertain domains such as the human body. This will enable robots that can use this internal representation and learn to decide or plan their next actions to accomplish a useful task in a safe way.

About us

QUT is a major Australian university with a global outlook and a 'real world' focus. We are an ambitious and collaborative institution that seeks to equip our students and graduates with the skills they will need in an increasingly disrupted and challenged world.

The School of Electrical Engineering and Robotics plays a significant role in almost every aspect of a modern lifestyle, supplying the energy and intelligence for the technology that helps us to manage and understand the world around us. We work to ensure safe, affordable and environmentally responsible supply and consumption of electricity. We make sense of the world’s data streaming in from cameras, devices, and sensors. Our expertise in Robotics, Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Wireless Communications and Signal Processing allows us to develop systems to automatically interpret the world, its people and their activities from diverse visual and audio sources. We create cutting edge robotic systems that interact with the real world, operating autonomously in our natural and built environments to benefit industries such as health, transport, manufacturing, mining and agriculture. We are committed to the education of Electrical Engineers both as undergraduates and postgraduates, and work with partner Schools to deliver leading edge Mechatronic, Software and Aerospace Engineering education.



What you need to succeed

Completion of a PhD in a relevant discipline preferably in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Vision or Machine Learning.
 Demonstrated expertise and understanding of the foundations of machine learning, such as Deep neural architectures, Transformers, Vision-Language Models/Actions, Decision-Making, and Informed Learning.
You understand the mathematical and statistical foundations of deep learning. You are driven and excited to constantly learn to understand new techniques and keep up with a quickly evolving field.
Substantial proficiency with using Python and deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch, and regularly work and develop in Linux. Ideally, you have a working knowledge of Git.
A demonstrated track record of publishing in the leading conferences and/or journals in robotics, computer vision, or machine learning.
Demonstrated written communication skills, including the ability to maintain accurate records, and experience in summarising scientific data in reports.
Demonstrated experience in all aspects of the research process, including ethical standards in research confidentiality and an ability to protect intellectual property.
Demonstrated commitment to equity, diversity and Indigenous Australian engagement.




Life at QUT

We're committed to building a culture that fosters connection between people and purpose. Beyond personal and professional fulfillment, a career at QUT offers:

A healthy work-life balance with a blend of on-campus and off-campus work arrangements for applicable positions. 
Competitive remuneration with up to 17% superannuation and leave loading of 17.5%. 
Generous parental leave including primary carer parental leave of up to 26 weeks (including 17% super). 
Stay healthy with Fitness Passport and enjoy discounted rates on private health insurance. 
Purchased Leave Scheme - up to 8 extra weeks. 
Salary Packaging Scheme for additional superannuation contributions, vehicle leasing, and on-campus services such as childcare, parking, and gyms. 
Numerous opportunities for professional development including leadership programs and workshops, and our study assistance scheme.
Commitment to our Indigenous Australian staff  through initiatives such as the Indigenous Australian Staff Network, Cultural and Ceremonial Leave, Staff Development Workshops, and representation on university committees. 
Explore more benefits of life at QUT.


Belong at QUT 

We are guided by our values of Ambition, Integrity, Inclusiveness, Innovation, and Academic Freedom and our QUT Connections in our mission to inspire and shape the next generation of change-makers. 


As part of our commitment to fostering an inclusive workplace, we proudly support our Indigenous Australians Employment Strategy, aiming for an Indigenous Australian workforce participation of 3.6%. Title: Data Engineer - Azure, Fabric & Data Pipelines
Description:
Join us at a pivotal moment of growth

Box Hill Institute is entering a significant period of growth and transformation, with major investment underway across our IT and AI implementation space. We're building capability, embedding smarter ways of working, and leveraging emerging technologies to support our people, students and future workforce.

APPLY NOW and be part of what's next.

Shape the future of data and AI at Box Hill Institute

Join a purpose-driven organisation where your work directly supports learners, educators and communities. As a Data Engineer, you'll design, build and operate secure, scalable data pipelines that power analytics, reporting and AI across Box Hill Institute. Working with Microsoft Fabric and Azure, you'll create trusted data assets that enable confident, data-driven decisions.

If you thrive on solving complex technical challenges, love working collaboratively and want to make your mark in a modern, evolving data environment - this is the opportunity for you.

What You'll Do

Design, build and operate data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric and Azure using approved ETL/ELT patterns.
Deliver reliable pipelines, feature sets, embeddings and curated datasets to support analytics and AI workloads.
Manage Fabric operations including workloads, schedules, connectors, configurations, secrets and version control.
Ensure data quality, governance and security through validation, secure coding, access controls and metadata management.
Maintain documentation including runbooks, schemas, data dictionaries, lineage and technical procedures.
Collaborate with BI/Analytics, Applications, Cyber Security, Infrastructure and AI teams on integrated solutions and Agile initiatives.
Provide operational support by triaging tickets, resolving incidents, clarifying requirements, supporting UAT, training and BAU transition.

What You'll Bring

Proven experience building and running scalable data pipelines for analytics and AI.
Strong ETL/ELT skills across varied data formats.
Hands-on capability with Microsoft Azure, Microsoft Fabric and modern integration tools (APIs, event streams, iPaaS).
Solid engineering foundations - Git, CI/CD, testing, logging and monitoring.
A clear understanding of data quality, governance and security practices.
Confidence in production support, incident resolution and performance optimisation.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify technical concepts for non-technical audiences.

Qualifications/Experience

A relevant qualification in IT, Computer Science, Information Systems or Data Engineering/Analytics (Azure/Fabric certifications a bonus).
Demonstrated experience designing and operating scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT
Hands-on skills with Azure data services, Microsoft Fabric and modern integration tools (APIs, event streams, iPaaS).

Why work with us?

At Box Hill Institute, we offer a supportive and rewarding work environment with benefits that are designed to help you thrive both professionally and personally:

Flexible work arrangements
On-site parking
Generous leave options such as Emergency response leave and up to 16 weeks paid Parental Leave
Health & wellbeing supports and resources, including FREE flu-vaccinations, FREE skin checks, FREE health checks and corporate health insurance discounts
Financial perks like novated leasing and salary packaging
Unique campus experiences to support student learning
Exclusive staff discounts on CAE/Box Hill Institute short courses for staff and their immediate family

APPLY NOW

All applications must be submitted online and include a cover letter and current resume.

For specific enquiries relating to this position, please contact it-•••••••@boxhill.edu.au

Applications close: 5:00pm, Friday 12th June 2026

Title: AI Consulting | Graduate
Description:
Entry Level Role in AI Consulting

Help design and build innovative client solutions

Work with cutting-edge AI models and tools

Launch your consulting career with hands-on experience and sales exposure

About the Opportunity

We’re on the hunt for a Graduate Consultant who’s eager to dive into problem-solving, client engagement, and sales, ready to kick off an exciting career. In this dynamic role, you’ll be a key part of our client-facing team, working alongside business leaders across various functions to analyse their processes, implement innovative solutions, and drive commercial outcomes. Your enthusiasm, willingness to learn, and ability to deliver top-notch service will set you up for a fast track to leadership.

About Us

Zudello is an Australian automation tech company specialising in using AI to extract data from different systems and documents, enabling end-to-end automation of business processes. Our Software-as-a-Service platform is used by businesses across a wide range of sectors, including Manufacturing, Hospitality, Health, and Not-for-Profit.

What You'll Do

Understand Client Needs: Join discovery sessions to learn about and document client requirements, helping design tailored solutions across various business functions.

Solution Rollout: Support the delivery and implementation of software solutions, ensuring smooth integration and configuration.

Customisation Collaboration: Work with the tech team to tailor solutions based on client needs, learning as you go.

Client Engagement & Sales: Actively manage client accounts, learning consulting and sales skills simultaneously to build strong relationships and drive business growth.

Project Support: Assist in managing implementation projects, helping keep them on track and within budget.

Onboarding and Go-Live: Help guide clients through onboarding, testing, and go-live processes.

User Documentation: Contribute to creating clear documentation to educate end-users on application features, best practices, and troubleshooting tips.

Quality Assurance: Assist with testing and quality checks on software applications, including functional, regression, and performance testing.

What We’re After

Recent graduate with a degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, STEM or a related field

Outstanding academic performance (high GPA)

Highly driven personality with a passion for taking on calculated risks and seizing growth opportunities

Keen interest in business strategy and processes (e.g., operations, supply chain, finance, HR, or procurement)

Confidence in communicating with clients and working in a team

Ability to think logically, learn quickly, and manage tasks effectively

Bonus: Any exposure to ERP, CRM, or enterprise systems (e.g., NetSuite, Salesforce, MYOB, Xero) through coursework or internships

Bonus: Interest in SaaS, AI and B2B Sales

Why Choose Zudello?

Fast-Track to Leadership: Unlike traditional consulting firms, we offer a quicker path to leadership roles, avoiding the 10-year technical specialist trap.

Consulting + Sales Hybrid: Gain early exposure to both technical consulting and commercial skills, equipping you with the sales expertise that’s increasingly critical in an AI-driven world.

Hands-On Experience: Work with the latest AI and automation tools in real business settings.

Mentorship & Growth: Learn from a clever, collaborative team that values curiosity and initiative, with support to transition from uni to the workplace.

Diverse Exposure: Engage with a wide range of industries, projects, and clients.

Shape the Future: Help shape how a fast-moving product is implemented and improved.

At Zudello, we know starting your career can feel daunting, and there’s no such thing as the 'perfect graduate.' The list of responsibilities and “ideal” requirements might make you wonder, “Can I do this?” We’re looking for driven graduates ready to take on challenges and grow with us. If you reckon this role is a great fit, even if you’re still building your experience, we encourage you to apply. We’ll support you in developing your skills and career as you grow and learn with us.

Your enthusiasm, potential, and drive to develop the skills, knowledge, and experience needed for this consulting and sales hybrid role are what we want to hear from you. Tell us why you’re excited to jump into the world of consulting, automation, and client success!

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Classification: ANU Senior Manager 1 (Administration)
Salary package: $ 138,182 - $ 144,783 per annum plus 17% superannuation
Terms: Full-time, Continuing

 

Position Description and Selection Criteria:  PD&PEWER_SM1_Senior Data Scientist.pdf

 

The Position

Operating under broad strategic guidance, the Senior Data Scientist is responsible for analysing data to draw insights, patterns, and conclusions that will enable the University to improve its processes or to develop new strategies. The role is required to deliver technical leadership in the development of advanced analytic solutions using statistical models, data visualization, business intelligence, machine learning, and artificial intelligence while ensuring data quality, integrity, and privacy.

The role will also be responsible for building and leading a team of Data Scientist Interns seeking to gain relevant skills and experience in data science that align with their course requirements. These interns will provide computer programming solutions and data management support for various analyses, particularly for the University's data analytics improvement and automation initiatives.

 

The Person

You are an experienced data scientist with a solid background working in complex organization(s). You bring demonstrated experience in conducting statistical analysis, developing predictive models, data visualization, and AI/machine learning solutions, and communicating findings to non-technical stakeholders.

You have proven people-management skills, with the ability to lead, coach, and mentor data science interns. Your strong analytical and problem-solving skills enable you to make informed decisions and provide trusted advice to senior stakeholders.

You are an excellent communicator, confident engaging with diverse audiences and delivering presentations, and skilled in building collaborative relationships across organisational boundaries. Experience in higher education will be highly regarded.

 

For further information

Please contact Irwan Krisna, Associate Director, Academic Performance and Productivity Analytics (APPA) E: Irw•••••••••@anu.edu.au

The University actively encourages applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. For more information on employment opportunities, contact our Indigenous Employment Consultant on indigenous.employment@anu.edu.au 

ANU values diversity and inclusion and is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to people of all backgrounds and identities.  For more information about staff equity at ANU, click here. For enquiries about the application process, or if you require reasonable adjustment to apply for this role, please contact the hiring manager on the above listed details.

 

Application information

In order to apply for this role please make sure that you upload the following documents:

A statement addressing the selection criteria.
A current curriculum vitae (CV) which includes the names and contact details of at least three referees (preferably including a current or previous supervisor). If your CV does not include referees you can complete these online when prompted in the application form.

 

Applications which do not address the selection criteria may not be considered for the position.


Please note: The successful candidate must have rights to live and work in this country and will be required to undergo a background check during the recruitment process. An offer of employment is conditional on satisfactory results.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Senior Data Scientist | NSW Government | Contract

Our client is a major NSW Government agency seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join a high-impact Technology and Communications function on an initial 12-month contract engagement.

This is a genuinely hands-on senior role for someone who thinks statistically first and reaches for tools second. You will work across complex datasets to design experiments, build and deploy classical ML models and apply optimisation techniques that drive real operational outcomes.


What you'll be doing:

Designing and developing classical ML models, statistical analyses and optimisation solutions
Applying operations research techniques to complex resource allocation problems
Working across large, complex and sensitive government datasets
Conducting experimental design and rigorous statistical analysis to derive meaningful insights
Communicating findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Contributing to data governance and best practice within a multidisciplinary team
Working independently when given a task while collaborating with the broader team as needed
What you'll need:

Strong statistical foundations, experimental design, hypothesis testing, inferential statistics and the ability to infer meaning from data rather than just apply models
Proven classical ML experience across ensemble methods, time series, Bayesian methods or optimisation modelling
Demonstrated ability to lead your own research or projects from brief through to delivery
Strong experience with Python.
Experience working with large, complex datasets in a regulated or enterprise environment
Australian Citizen, Permanent Resident or NZ Citizen with a current NZ Passport (mandatory)
Ability to obtain and maintain a NSW Police Force security clearance
Highly regarded:

Optimisation modelling experience -- operations research, combinatorial optimisation or resource allocation
Government, law enforcement or highly regulated industry background
Academic or research background with published work
R proficiency alongside Python
For more information, send your CV to cia••••••••@pra.com.au

Title: Graduate Digital Technology
Description:

Rotation of operational and technical roles, you will be provided exposure to allow you to advance your abilities and career across the duration of the Graduate Program.

Experience and/or strong interest in working in mining

The chance to be part of an organisation that values innovation and collaboration

Ready to take the next step in your career? Apply now!

Title: Research Engineer - Software
Description:
RESEARCH ENGINEER - SOFTWARE

OncoRes Medical was founded at the intersection of medicine, science and humanity to improve the accuracy of breast-conserving surgery and eliminate the physical, psychological and economic burdens associated with repeat operations.

Our unique, informal culture champions vision, restlessness and humanity. Knowing we have an open and supportive team means each of us has the freedom to take responsibility and ownership. We have a shared passion to work restlessly, pioneering change to re-imagine the world, while remaining grounded in our commitment to science and quality.

Our passion for helping patients focuses us on the patient. We believe that all breast cancer patients deserve the opportunity to move beyond their breast cancer surgery knowing that all the cancer has been removed, the first time. In everything we do, we are thinking about how we can inch closer to a world free from re-excisions... We are going BEYOND BREAST CANCER.

Overview:

We are seeking a candidate to work within a multi-disciplinary team geared towards research and development within OncoRes Medical. The role will primarily focus on proof-of-concept development of software tools for data acquisition, processing, modelling and analysis to verify and improve performance of our medical imaging device.

You’ll be working closely with a small, collaborative team in a fast-paced startup environment, where your contribution will have a visible and meaningful impact. Based in Nedlands, this role is offered as a 1.0 FTE fixed-term contract for 12 months.

Role:

Develop proof of concept tools for interaction with device components and test hardware.

Develop data acquisition and analysis tools for assembly and quality control test protocols.

Model device performance to inform device configuration and design.

Perform data analysis and system modelling to assess current performance and identify potential improvements in device configuration and design.

Ensure project deliverables and tasks are technically excellent and completed in a timely fashion. Interact with technical leadership to plan, manage, and report results of experiments, tests, and proof-of-concept development.

Where necessary, generate design documentation in accordance with IEC 62304 and OncoRes Medical Quality Management Systems requirements based on ISO 13485 and ISO 14971 standards, and ensure personal compliance with quality system procedures.

Maintain and develop the technical capabilities necessary to provide technical solutions to OncoRes Medical and customer needs. This may include, but is not limited to: Technical Courses, conferences, specific software training, university courses, etc.

Support the development of intellectual property for deliverables and tasks. Understand the intellectual property process. Ensure the intellectual property is established, harvested, characterised, and protected.

Qualifications and Experience:

Minimum Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Mechatronics, Engineering/Science, or equivalent degree.

3 to 10 years’ experience in a high-technology or research-oriented role is highly desirable.

Experience with software development in Python, C/C++, or other relevant programming languages. Experience with software development for medical devices, or other safety critical systems would be highly regarded.

Relevant experience with design, analysis, or implementation of optical or other imaging systems would be highly desirable.  

Ability to plan, prioritize, and coordinate the completion of complex deliverables and tasks, must be highly flexible, have a hands-on approach and be willing to take initiative.

Ability to effectively communicate complex information through written and verbal means as well as constructively communicate and collaborate with colleagues and external parties.

Ability to work with honesty and transparency and commitment to doing what’s best for our patients, customers, and company.

Qualified candidates must have permanent working rights in Australia.

Benefits:

Comprehensive and competitive remuneration package.

A flexible and fun workplace where we understand that your life is more than your job.

A rare opportunity to work closely alongside world-class colleagues and mentors with many collective years' of experience in all aspects of medical device development and commercialisation. All of us share a passion for working collaboratively where every voice is heard, and your personal and professional development is a high priority.

OncoRes is committed to building and fostering an inclusive, diverse workplace. We believe the differing perspectives and experiences each of us brings inevitably increases innovation and teamwork, flowing into positive tangible outcomes.

How to apply

Please apply online via SEEK and ensure you attach the following documents:

Cover letter addressed to James Anstie, Research Program Manager, outlining your suitability for the role.

CV

Applications close: Friday 22 May 2026
This position is only open to applicants with relevant rights to work in Australia

Please note applications will only be accepted via SEEK

Description:
