Title: Data Scientist
Description:
The role is to support the Strategic Pricing Manager and partner with Sales, Product, Marketing, Finance, and IT to transform complex datasets into meaningful insights and scalable pricing solutions.

This is achieved by leveraging advanced data analytics, statistical modelling, and machine learning techniques to generate actionable insights that drive commercial, operational, and strategic decision-making within the pricing function.



The role:


Use tools such as SQL, Python, Power BI, and other analytics platforms to extract, analyse, and interpret large datasets .
Identify trends, patterns, and opportunities to improve pricing, sales performance, customer segmentation, and operational efficiency .
Develop, validate, and deploy predictive models including forecasting, segmentation, and optimisation models .
Collaborate with IT and Data Engineering teams to design scalable data pipelines and automate analytical processes .
Design and maintain dashboards and reporting suites that provide performance visibility and key business metrics .
Support strategic initiatives through advanced modelling, scenario analysis, and clear communication of technical insights to stakeholders .

Who are you?


Demonstrated experience in Data Science, Advanced Analytics, or Statistical Modelling .
University degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or related discipline .
Strong knowledge of statistical methods, predictive modelling, and machine learning techniques .
Advanced proficiency in Python (e.g., pandas, scikit-learn), SQL, and Power BI or similar BI tools .
Experience working with structured and unstructured data .
Proven ability to collaborate with business stakeholders and communicate insights effectively .

About Motion

At Motion, our core product lines embrace bearings, power transmission, sealing, fasteners, driveshafts, drives and motors, belting, fluid power, engineering services, lifting and safety equipment, tools, and maintenance products . With stocked lines exceeding 350,000, we have a presence in almost all industrial and trade sectors from agriculture and automotive to mining and transport in Australia .

We operate across Australia, New Zealand, Indonesia, and Singapore and are part of a larger group, GPC, where opportunities are endless for a motivated individual.

We value relationships and foster trusted partnerships across all levels of our business. We focus on continuous improvement and sustainable growth, driven by long-term investment in our people, systems, and processes .

So, if you are interested in joining our group, don't wait - apply now and let us help you empower your career with us!

MOTION is an equal opportunity employer.

You must be either an Australian Citizen or a holder of an Australian Permanent Residency Visa to be eligible to apply for this position .

If this sounds like the opportunity for you, click "Apply for this job" and submit both your resume and cover letter. Please note only short-listed candidates will be contacted .

Title: Staff Data Scientist - AI & NLP
Description:
Are you a data science expert passionate about AI innovation and stakeholder influence? We’re seeking a Staff Data Scientist to drive cutting-edge AI advancements while working closely with executives and non-technical stakeholders to shape AI adoption and impact.
This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of technical excellence and business strategy, ensuring AI solutions deliver real value across the organisation.

About the Role
As a Lead or Principal Data Scientist, you will play a crucial role in developing and deploying machine learning models, optimising AI performance, and influencing key business decisions. Your expertise in NLP, LLMs, and MLOps will drive innovation, while your ability to communicate complex AI concepts to executives will ensure AI initiatives align with business objectives.
This is a high-visibility role where you’ll work across teams, bridging the gap between technical execution and business strategy—helping senior stakeholders understand the power of AI and how to apply it effectively.

Key Responsibilities
AI Strategy & Business Alignment – Work closely with executives and business leaders to integrate AI-driven solutions into company strategy.
Technical Leadership – Design, develop, and optimise machine learning models, with a focus on NLP and large-scale AI applications.
Executive Stakeholder Influence – Translate complex AI concepts into clear, actionable insights for non-technical leaders.
MLOps & AI Engineering – Implement best practices for model deployment, monitoring, and performance optimisation at scale.
Data-Driven Decision Making – Drive high-impact AI initiatives by leveraging advanced analytics on structured and unstructured data.
Research & Innovation – Stay ahead of AI trends, applying cutting-edge techniques to enhance business performance and operational efficiency.
What You’ll Bring
8+ years of experience (Staff Level) in data science, AI, and machine learning, with strong expertise in NLP and LLMs.
A track record of engaging with C-suite and senior business stakeholders, influencing AI strategy and execution.
Expert-level Python and SQL skills, with deep experience in building and deploying machine learning models at scale.
Hands-on experience with MLOps best practices, cloud-based AI solutions, and large-scale data processing.
A strategic mindset, with the ability to translate AI advancements into business impact.
Exceptional communication and influencing skills, able to simplify complex concepts for non-technical audiences.
What’s in It for You?
Work at the forefront of AI innovation, shaping strategy at a high-growth tech company.
Partner directly with executives and business leaders to drive enterprise-wide AI transformation.
Join a team that values curiosity, technical excellence, and real-world AI applications.
Hybrid work model (Sydney-based, 2-3 days in-office).

Title: Statistical Programmer (CDISC – SDTM & ADaM)
Description:
Datapharm Australia is a leading contract research organisation (CRO), renowned for providing high-quality clinical trial management and statistical analysis for pharmaceutical and biotech companies both locally and globally. Established in 1987, Datapharm has built a strong reputation for excellence in statistical services, and we are now seeking a Statistical Programmer to join our growing team, which is an exciting opportunity for growth and professional development.

As part of the Datapharm team, you will develop and maintain CDISC-compliant SDTM and ADaM datasets, support analysis programming, and contribute to clinical trial data management and reporting. If you're ready to take the next step in your career, you will have the opportunity to work on ongoing clinical trials and apply your expertise and skills to real-world challenges while making a significant impact on the success of our projects and clients.

Key Responsibilities:

Build and maintain SDTM and ADaM datasets in line with CDASH and CDISC standards.

Ensure traceability, quality, and regulatory compliance (21 CFR Part 11).

Program tables, listings, and figures to support statistical analysis.

Perform data validation and plausibility checks.

Support clinical trial database setup, data capture, and reporting.

Collaborate closely with statisticians, other programmers, and data management teams.

Provide documentation, training, and mentorship where required.

Lead improvements to internal processes, ensuring continuous enhancement of programming methodologies and practices.

Qualifications & Requirements:

Essential:

Proficiency in SAS programming; experience with R, Python, or other relevant programming languages is a plus.

Knowledge of CDISC, SDTM, and ADaM implementation requirements.

Qualifications in programming or related field.

Familiarity with database design and data handling principles.

The ability to write clean, reusable, and documented code.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, especially in preparing clinical study reports and regulatory documents.

An eye for detail and commitment to accuracy and quality in all statistical work.

Strong organisational and time management skills, with the ability to manage deadlines and prioritise tasks.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively within a team.

Desirable:

Knowledge and experience with other programming languages and training AI models.

Experience with version control systems (Git, Mercurial, or similar).

Post-graduate qualifications in programming or related field.

Title: Software Engineer – Full Stack & AI Systems
Description:

StudyNet is an AI-powered Australian EdTech platform transforming how students, counsellors, and institutions connect. Title: Forward Deployed AI Engineer
Description:
Forward-Deployed AI Engineer

Contract / Sydney / Hybrid 

Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

There is a new breed of engineer reshaping how enterprise AI gets built — and it doesn’t look like a traditional consultant or a traditional developer. Pioneered by frontier AI labs and the firms that deploy them, the Forward-Deployed Engineer is part builder, part product thinker, part trusted advisor — embedded directly inside an enterprise client to design, prototype, and ship Anthropic Claude-powered solutions that traditional delivery models can’t produce.

Galileo Search is building Australia’s first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. We’re assembling a small, elite group of Forward-Deployed AI Engineers — the people we send into our most complex enterprise engagements when standard delivery isn’t enough.

What you’ll do on client engagements

Embed directly inside enterprise client teams — Big 4 banks, federal agencies, top ASX corporates, healthcare networks — to translate business pain into shipped Claude solutions
Move fluidly between engineering, product discovery, and stakeholder consulting — you write the code AND you help decide what to build
Build rapid prototypes that demonstrate value in days, then harden them into production-grade Claude integrations and deployments
Work directly with business unit leaders, and end users — no layer of abstraction, no handoff to “the implementation team”
Identify the highest-leverage AI use cases inside a complex enterprise, prioritise them ruthlessly, and personally deliver the first wave
Coordinate other Galileo specialists (Solutions Architects, Integration Engineers, Data Engineers, Cybersecurity & AI Governance) when broader capabilities are needed 
Hold the line on quality and security in environments where the stakes — regulatory, reputational, financial — are at their highest
What you bring

5+ years of software engineering experience
Production-grade Python or TypeScript experience 
A track record of shipping software that real users use — not just prototypes, but deployed systems running in regulated, high-stakes environments
Strong product instincts — you can identify what to build, not just how to build it, and you push back on scope that won’t deliver value
Ability to operate independently in ambiguous environments where the requirements aren’t written down 
Comfort across the full stack — from MCP servers and enterprise auth (OAuth, SAML, SSO) to data pipelines and quick UI prototypes when needed
Calm under pressure — you can present to a board steering committee on Tuesday and ship code that same week 
Bonus (not required)

Deep familiarity with Claude API, MCP protocol, and Cowork — at the level where you’ve written your own servers and skills
Experience in regulated industries (financial services, government, healthcare, critical infrastructure, defence)
Track record running enterprise discovery sprints, design sprints, or AI value workshops with senior leadership
AWS, Azure, or GCP architecture certification, or equivalent depth in enterprise cloud
Comfort with enterprise platforms (SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Oracle) as integration targets
What Galileo offers

Great ongoing contract engagements reflecting the seniority, scarcity, and autonomy of the role
Free Anthropic certification — go deeper than the standard certification with additional materials and support
The Forward-Deployed Engineer model exists because enterprise AI is too important and too fast-moving for traditional delivery. Clients need someone who can think with them, build with them, and ship with them — in the room, not on a status call three weeks from now. The engineers who develop this capability now will define enterprise AI delivery in Australia for the next decade. We’re assembling that first cohort, and the bench is deliberately small.

To apply:
Share your CV, a brief overview of an AI or software project you shipped end-to-end (from discovery through production). We value evidence of shipped work over certifications.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Pro-Vice-Chancellor (Research Infrastructure)
Full-time, fixed-term position for up to 2 years
Base salary will be in the range $117,958.12 - $132,247.57 + super (HEW Level 8)
Based at our Herston Campus location

About This Opportunity

This is an exciting opportunity for a skilled and inquisitive Data Engineer to join a collaborative research and digital health environment that sits at the intersection of The University of Queensland and Queensland Health. Working within the Data Science Collaborative Research Platform and embedded in the Queensland Digital Health Centre, this role plays a critical part in delivering high‑impact research infrastructure that enables secure, scalable and meaningful use of health data. You will contribute to nationally significant initiatives including the Australian Health Data Evidence Network and the National Infrastructure for Federated Learning in Digital Health, supporting researchers to translate complex clinical data into insights that improve health outcomes.

Key responsibilities will include:

Designing, developing and maintaining robust ETL and ELT data pipelines to support research and analytics initiatives.
Extracting, transforming and managing data from integrated electronic medical record systems to meet research and governance requirements.
Providing expert technical advice and support to researchers and stakeholders across UQ and Queensland Health.
Producing high‑quality technical documentation, data lineage artefacts and clear explanations for technical and non‑technical audiences.

About You

You will bring a strong foundation in data engineering and a collaborative, purposeful mindset, along with:

A Bachelor's degree or higher qualification in Computer Science, Information Technology or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience designing and maintaining data pipelines using SQL and modern data engineering practices.
Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and experience working with relational and non‑relational databases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms, preferably Microsoft Azure, and contemporary DevOps or CI/CD practices.
Experience working with sensitive or confidential data and a clear understanding of secure data handling principles.
Strong documentation, communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into plain English.
Experience with tools such as Databricks, DBT or PowerBI, and exposure to clinical or healthcare datasets, will be highly regarded.

About UQ

As part of the UQ community, you'll have the opportunity to work alongside some of the brightest minds from around the world, united by a shared purpose to create change together. UQ is a globally recognised, values‑led institution where collaboration, curiosity and ambition are encouraged, and where professional staff play a vital role in enabling research, teaching and community impact.

In 2025, UQ announced a major milestone in digital health research infrastructure, strengthening national capabilities in secure health data sharing and analytics. This work directly aligns with the mission of teams such as the Data Science Collaborative Research Platform, creating opportunities for staff to contribute to research that has real‑world impact across Australia's health system.

Enjoy a range of benefits and rewards, including up to 26 weeks paid parental and primary care leave, 17.5% leave loading, flexible and hybrid working options, health and wellbeing programs, access to discounted study opportunities, on‑campus childcare, affordable parking, and salary packaging.


Interested?

Pre‑employment checks may include verification of the right to work in Australia, qualifications, criminal history checks and other integrity and conduct requirements.

You must maintain unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this appointment to apply. Employer sponsored work rights are not available for this appointment.

Title: Data Test Automation Engineer
Description:
About the Role
Looking for an ambitious and detail oriented Associate Data Test Automation Engineer to join a Data Engineering team. You will support automated data quality checks across our cloud based data platform and contribute to the uplift of reliable, consistent and well governed data for use across the business.

What You Will Do

Execution and Maintenance: Execute and maintain existing data test automation scripts written in Python, ensuring reliable data validation across pipeline stages.
Data Validation: Write and execute complex SQL queries, including T SQL and ANSI SQL, to perform targeted data validation and reconciliation between source systems and the BigQuery data warehouse.
Pipeline Support: Assist senior engineers in integrating automated test suites into the CI CD pipeline to monitor and report on build quality.
Test Case Development: Translate business requirements and data modelling specifications into detailed and executable test cases and test scripts for data quality assurance.
Release Support: Support User Acceptance Testing and Production Verification Testing during data platform releases.
Documentation: Maintain clear and current documentation for data quality rules, test cases and execution results.

What We Are Looking For
     Core Data and Cloud Fundamentals

One to three years of experience in quality assurance with a focus on testing ETL or ELT pipelines and data warehouse environments
Strong proficiency in SQL with the ability to write complex T SQL queries for validation and comparison of large datasets
Foundational experience with cloud data warehouses, preferably BigQuery or similar platforms such as Snowflake
Solid understanding of data warehouse fundamentals, including dimensional modelling, fact and dimension tables, and the differences between OLAP and OLTP
     Automation and Programming

Hands on experience with Python for scripting and data manipulation, ideally with pandas and google cloud bigquery libraries
Basic exposure to testing frameworks such as pytest
Understanding of test automation concepts and why automation is critical for data quality in a modern CI CD environment
Desirable Skills

Exposure to CI CD tools such as GitHub Actions or Jenkins
Basic understanding of data modelling concepts such as Slowly Changing Dimensions
Interest in the use of Generative AI and BigQuery ML for synthetic test data generation

Title: Data Processing Analyst & Data Solutions Engineer
Description:
The Social Research Centre (SRC) is Australia's trusted partner for rigorous, ethical and high-quality social research and evaluation. Our work informs public policy and programs across health, education, employment and social services, and supports better decision-making that reflects the experiences of people across Australia.

We deliver nationally significant projects at scale, combining deep methodological expertise with close collaboration between researchers, statisticians and data specialists.




 

About the role

We're looking for people to join our Data Operations team to support the delivery of high-quality research projects through strong data preparation, processing and analysis.

The work sits at the intersection of data analysis and data engineering, turning complex and messy datasets into clean, structured, analysis-ready outputs. Depending on the position, you'll either contribute to these workflows and quality checks (Data Processing Analyst) or lead the design and implementation of end-to-end data solutions (Data Solutions Engineer) so our researchers and project teams can deliver robust, reliable insights to clients.

If you enjoy working hands-on with data, solving messy problems and building efficient, repeatable processes, this is a great fit.

Please note: this is not a machine learning, AI or commercial data science role. The focus is on research data operations - data preparation, processing, pipeline development and quality assurance - in support of large-scale social research projects.

 

Location and hybrid working

This role is based in Melbourne (VIC).

SRC operates under a hybrid working model, which involves a mix of working from the Melbourne office and from home. For these roles, employees should expect to attend the Melbourne office at least three days per week.

Please do not apply if you are seeking a fully remote role.

 

What you'll do

- Prepare, clean and transform large and complex datasets (survey and administrative data)
- Build and maintain repeatable data processing workflows and pipelines
- Combine data from multiple sources into coherent, documented, analysis-ready datasets
- Support researchers and project teams with data extraction, validation and quality checks
- Conduct ad hoc analysis and generate structured outputs for reporting
- Identify opportunities to automate and improve data workflows
- Work with tools such as SQL, Python/R, Excel and Git
- Work closely with researchers and project teams to ensure data solutions are accurate, practical, well-documented and delivered on time




 

What you'll bring

Essential
- Proficiency in Python or R for data manipulation and processing - this is a non-negotiable requirement for both roles
- Strong technical experience in data wrangling, transformation and analysis
- Experience handling large or complex datasets
- A structured, detail-oriented approach to data quality and validation
- Ability to translate unclear or messy data problems into practical solutions
- Strong communication skills - working proactively with project teams to clarify requirements, surface downstream impacts and support smooth handovers

Nice to have
- Experience working with survey data or in a research environment
- Experience working with online survey platforms
- Exposure to data pipeline tools or cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Snowflake)
- Familiarity with data governance, privacy or compliance frameworks
- Experience creating dashboards or reporting outputs (e.g. Power BI)
- Experience using AI tools to boost productivity and data workflows

 

We are recruiting for two positions in Data Operations:

Data Processing Analyst
An entry-level role, best suited to someone early in their career who enjoys structured problem-solving and hands-on data preparation. The role focuses on preparing, cleaning and validating survey and administrative datasets so they are accurate, consistent and ready for analysis and reporting. Working alongside senior team members, you'll build repeatable processing workflows in Python/R and SQL, run quality checks, produce structured outputs for project teams and clients, and help document data processes and requirements.

Data Solutions Engineer
A mid-level, hands-on role best suited to someone with demonstrated experience designing scalable data workflows and leading technical delivery. You'll design and deliver end-to-end data solutions for complex research programs, lead the development of robust pipelines and automation, integrate data from multiple systems, set standards for data quality and governance, and partner with researchers, operations and clients to translate requirements into reliable, maintainable workflows and reporting-ready datasets.

 

Why join us

- Work on meaningful projects that inform policy, research and social outcomes - a rare chance to use your skills for real social good that positively impacts Australian society
- Be part of a collaborative team of researchers, analysts and data specialists
- Opportunity to shape and improve how we manage and process data at scale
- Work in a passionate team investing in continuous improvement of standards, automation and cross-functional collaboration
- Flexible working arrangements and a supportive team environment
- Paid parental leave, wellbeing initiatives and access to SRC's standard employee benefits

 

How to apply

Please submit the following via the advertised recruitment channels (Seek, LinkedIn or SRC's online application portal):

- Your CV, and
- A cover letter (maximum one page) addressing:

1. Which programming language are you most proficient in, and how do you use it in your work? Please provide a concrete example.
2. Tell us about an application or process you have developed: what did it involve, and how did you test that it was accurate? We welcome a link to an example of your code (e.g. via GitHub) if available.
3. Confirm that you are willing to work in a hybrid (in-office and WFH) setting, attending the Melbourne CBD office at least three days per week.

Applications submitted outside the advertised process cannot be accepted or considered.

Shortlisted applicants will be required to undertake a two-hour online exercise designed to gauge technical skills and know-how.

Please note that applications will be reviewed upon submission and we may start shortlisting candidates before the closing date.

Applications close Friday 29 May 2026.

 

Work rights

Title: Python Data Test Engineer
Company: Cognizant Technology Solutions Australia Pty Ltd

Title: Junior Software Developer
Description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Junior Software Developer (12‑month fixed-term contract) to join our Engineering R&D team in the In Vitro Diagnostics (IVD) space. This role is pivotal in developing, testing, and maintaining software for diagnostic instruments in accordance with medical device standards. You'll collaborate with engineers, scientists, and regulatory experts to create robust, scalable, and compliant solutions that improve diagnostic accuracy and patient outcomes.

KEY DUTIES

Design and develop software for IVD instruments, including desktop or cloud-based applications. 

Contribute to software development planning, including weekly sprint planning. 

Implement software in compliance with IEC 62304, ISO 13485, and other relevant regulatory standards. 

Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code using languages and frameworks such as C#, C++, .Net, Avalonia, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, etc. 

Collaborate with other disciplines (e.g. automation, product management, assay development, QA/RA) to define software requirements and system specifications. 

Participate in code reviews, software risk assessments, and hazard analysis, according to ISO 14971. 

Support verification and validation activities, including unit testing, system integration testing, and test protocol and report documentation. 

Respond to bug reports, troubleshoot issues, and implement bug fixes throughout the software development lifecycle. 

Contribute to technical documentation, including software development plans, software architecture, interface control documents, release notes, etc. 
 

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

Essential:

Firm grasp of software application fundamentals including architecture, algorithms, data structures, and design principles like SOC, SOLID, DRY, and YAGNI. 

Experience building software applications, preferably in a regulated environment. 

Experience using AI code tools but capable of working without AI support. 

Experience planning and prioritising tasks with competing priorities and taking responsibility for a fixed scope of work within a team setting. 

Proficient in C#. Familiar with .NET application frameworks. 

Attention to detail, willing to follow processes, quick to ask questions and learn. 

Available to travel to Mascot, Sydney for regular, hands-on, system testing. Desirable:

Experience developing software for automated laboratory systems or point-of-care diagnostics.

Understanding of molecular diagnostics, immunoassays, or clinical workflows.

Knowledge of cybersecurity requirements for medical devices (e.g., FDA premarket guidance).

Desirable: 

Familiarity with ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, IEC 62366. 

Experience developing software for automated systems or point-of-care devices. 

Understanding of molecular diagnostics, immunoassays, or clinical workflows.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer / AI Engineer
Description:
Senior Machine Learning Engineer / AI Engineer

About the Role This is an opportunity to join a high-impact AI team building real, customer-facing products at scale. You’ll sit at the intersection of data science and engineering, taking cutting-edge models from concept through to production and ensuring they perform reliably in real-world environments.
You’ll work across recommendation systems, dynamic pricing engines, and emerging GenAI and agent-based applications, helping shape how intelligent systems are delivered to millions of users.
This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving team, ideal for someone who enjoys building, shipping, and seeing their work drive measurable outcomes.
Key Responsibilities
Take machine learning models from POC through to robust, scalable production systems
Build and optimise end-to-end ML pipelines, including training, inference, and monitoring
Deploy and manage AI solutions in cloud environments, ideally GCP and Vertex AI
Develop and experiment with GenAI and agent-based systems, including customer and internal analytics use cases
Collaborate closely with Data Scientists to productionise models and improve performance
Implement best practices across CI/CD, version control, testing, and model lifecycle management
Continuously improve system performance, scalability, and reliability in production environments
What You’ll Bring
Proven experience deploying machine learning models into production environments
Strong Python and ML engineering capability, with experience building scalable systems
Experience working with APIs, microservices, and batch or real-time inference pipelines
Exposure to MLOps practices, including CI/CD, Docker, and monitoring frameworks
Experience working with GenAI, LLMs, or agent-based architectures
Cloud experience, GCP preferred, though AWS or Azure backgrounds are also valuable
A product mindset, focused on delivering real outcomes rather than just models
Why This Role
Work on AI systems that directly impact customer experience at scale
Strong focus on production, not just experimentation
Exposure to modern GenAI and agent-based architectures
Small, high-performing team with fast decision-making and real ownership
